radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT TORSO WITHOUT CONTRAST

INDICATION:   ___ with pleural effusions, PNA, evaluate for acute process

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector CT images of the chest, abdomen and pelvis were
acquired without intravenous contrast. Non-contrast scan has several
limitations in detecting vascular and parenchymal organ abnormalities,
including tumor detection.
Coronal and sagittal reformations were performed and reviewed on PACS.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 6.2 s, 68.4 cm; CTDIvol = 17.0 mGy (Body) DLP =
1,162.8 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,163 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Same-day chest radiograph.  CT Abdomen and Pelvis ___

FINDINGS: 

CHEST:

The thyroid is unremarkable.  There is no axillary or supraclavicular
adenopathy.  There are prominent mediastinal lymph nodes measuring up to 10 mm
in the lower prevascular station.  Heart size is moderately enlarged.  There
is no pericardial effusion.  Coronary artery calcifications are moderate.  The
thoracic aorta is normal in caliber with moderate atherosclerotic disease. 
The main pulmonary trunk is dilated up to 32 mm.

The airways are patent to the segmental level bilaterally.  There is mild
emphysematous change.  There are no pleural effusions.  There are bibasilar
airspace opacities, right greater than left.  There is no interlobular septal
thickening.  Note is made of a 6 mm perifissural left pulmonary nodule, likely
an intrapulmonary lymph node.

The thoracic esophagus is unremarkable.

ABDOMEN:

HEPATOBILIARY: The liver demonstrates homogeneous attenuation throughout. 
There is no evidence of focal lesions within the limitations of an unenhanced
scan.  There is no evidence of intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary
dilatation.  The gallbladder is within normal limits.

PANCREAS: The pancreas has normal attenuation throughout, without evidence of
focal lesions within the limitations of an unenhanced scan.  There is no
pancreatic ductal dilatation.  There is no peripancreatic stranding.

SPLEEN: The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout, without
evidence of focal lesions.

ADRENALS: The right and left adrenal glands are normal in size and shape.

URINARY: The kidneys atrophic.  There are bilateral simple renal cysts
measuring up to 7.1 cm on the right.  There is residual irregular enhancement
of the bilateral kidneys as well as contrast excretion on the left, suggesting
renal dysfunction.  There is a more focal abnormal area of renal enhancement
in the left lower pole measuring 2.1 cm (series 601b, image 40).  Bilateral
perinephric stranding is nonspecific.

GASTROINTESTINAL: There is a small hiatal hernia.  There is no bowel
obstruction.  There is no bowel wall thickening.  There is no intra-abdominal
free fluid or free air.

PELVIS: The urinary bladder and distal ureters are unremarkable.  There is no
free fluid in the pelvis.

REPRODUCTIVE ORGANS: Brachytherapy seeds are present in the pelvis.

LYMPH NODES: There are scattered retroperitoneal lymph nodes, but none that
are pathologically enlarged.  There is no pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

VASCULAR: There is no abdominal aortic aneurysm.  Moderate atherosclerotic
disease is noted.

BONES: There is a lucency extending through the anterior corner of the T9
vertebral body, concerning for fracture.  The posterior elements are intact.

SOFT TISSUES: There is a fat containing umbilical hernia.

IMPRESSION:


1. Bibasilar airspace opacities, concerning for infection.  Pulmonary edema
felt to be less likely as there are no pleural effusions or interlobular
septal thickening.
2. Fracture through the anteroinferior endplate of T9 vertebral body.  No
evidence of extension into the posterior elements.
3. Delayed contrast excretion, can be seen in the setting of renal failure.
4. Enhancement in the left lower pole of the kidney, nonemergent ultrasound is
recommended to evaluate for underlying lesion, if clinically indicated.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  Non-emergent renal ultrasound to evaluate the left kidney
if clinically indicated.

NOTIFICATION:   The findings were discussed with ___, M.D. by ___
___, M.D. on the telephone on ___ at 9:48 am, 5 minutes after
discovery of the findings.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  ___ male with AFib and tachypnea, recently on Coumadin.

COMPARISON:  None.

TECHNIQUE:  Contiguous helical acquisition through the chest was performed
with intravenous contrast.  Coronal, sagittal, and MIP images of the pulmonary
arteries were created.

FINDINGS:  The aorta opacifies normally without evidence of aortic dissection.
There is atherosclerotic disease of the descending aorta and aortic arch.  The
pulmonary arteries opacify normally without intraluminal thrombus.  There is
atherosclerotic disease involving the coronary arteries.  No mediastinal or
hilar lymphadenopathy is identified.  A 2-mm left lower lobe nodule is again
identified and unchanged since ___.  No focal consolidations are
identified within the lungs.   There are no pleural effusions or pneumothorax.
Median sternotomy wires are identified.  A dual-lead pacemaker is in place.

Images of the upper abdomen demonstrate a normal-appearing liver, spleen,
adrenal glands, kidneys, and pancreas.  Multilevel degenerative changes are
noted throughout the spine.

IMPRESSION:

1.  No acute pulmonary embolism or intrathoracic process.

2.  Stable-appearing 2-mm left lower lobe pulmonary nodule, unchanged since
___.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST WANDW/O CANDRECONS, NON-CORONARY

INDICATION:  History: ___ with left upper extremity deep venous thrombosis,
new hypoxia

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.
Reformatted coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal
intensity projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Total DLP (Body) = 420 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  PA and lateral chest dated ___

FINDINGS: 

The aorta and its major branch vessels are patent, with no evidence of
stenosis, occlusion, dissection, or aneurysmal formation.  There is no
evidence of penetrating atherosclerotic ulcer.  Moderate atherosclerosis of
the aorta is noted.  Evaluation of the heart demonstrates extensive coronary
artery calcifications as well as mild aortic valve calcifications to the is
moderate enlargement of the heart.

The pulmonary arteries are well opacified to the subsegmental level, with no
evidence of filling defect within the main, right, left, lobar, segmental or
subsegmental pulmonary arteries.  The main pulmonary artery is enlarged,
measuring up to 3.6 cm in greatest diameter.

There is no supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy. 
The thyroid gland is markedly enlarged with multiple nodules and extends into
the anterior mediastinum, causing mass effect on and marked narrowing of the
trachea to a transverse diameter of 6 mm.  These findings predominantly
involve the left lobe of the thyroid, which measures roughly 6.8 x 7.7 x 6.4
cm.

There is no evidence of pericardial effusion.  There is no pleural effusion.

Mild centrilobular emphysematous changes are seen at the apices.  Multiple
solid pulmonary nodules are noted bilaterally including left upper lobe
(3:81), left lower lobe (3:  130 and 168), and right lower lobe (3:160 and
161).  The largest of these measures up to 7 mm in the right lower lobe. The
airways are patent to the subsegmental level.  Atelectasis is noted in the
lingula and left upper lobe.

Limited images of the upper abdomen demonstrates a small hiatal hernia. 
Scattered sub cm hypodensities in the liver may reflect cysts or biliary
hamartomas, but not fully characterized.  Multiple renal hypodensities are
noted, likely cysts.  A splenic cyst is noted as well.  Marked atherosclerotic
calcifications are seen at the takeoff of the celiac artery with at least
moderate narrowing.

Multilevel degenerative changes are seen throughout the thoracic spine,
without significant vertebral body height loss.

IMPRESSION: 

1.   No evidence of pulmonary embolism or acute aortic abnormality.
2.  Multiple pulmonary nodules, the largest of which measures 7 mm.  If
clinically indicated, follow-up chest CT is suggested at ___ months.  If there
is no change, a second follow-up in ___ months and then at 24 months is
recommended if there is no change.
3.  Dilated main pulmonary artery likely reflective of pulmonary arterial
hypertension.
4.  Markedly enlarged thyroid gland with mediastinal extension and severe
narrowing of the trachea.
5.  Mild centrilobular emphysema.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  Multiple pulmonary nodules, the largest of which measures
7 mm.  If clinically indicated, follow-up chest CT is suggested at ___ months.
If there is no change, a second follow-up in ___ months and then at 24 months
is recommended if there is no change.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  New diagnosis of metastatic cancer with unknown primary.  Assess for
extent of lesions.  

COMPARISON:  CT abdomen and pelvis dated ___. 

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector CT of the torso was performed with intravenous and
oral contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformations were provided.  Images are
substantially degraded due to the patient's body habitus.  

FINDINGS:

CHEST: 

There are innumerable pulmonary nodules, which have an upper lobe
predilection, particularly in the left upper lobe.  The largest nodule
measures 6 mm in diameter (sequence 3 image 8).  Calcification is noted within
the right pleural cavity (sequence 3 image 24), which is likely related to the
patient's history of previous right lung surgery.  No pleural effusion.  No
pneumothorax. 

No significant mediastinal, axillary or hilar adenopathy.  Cardiac size is
normal.  No pericardial effusion. 

ABDOMEN: 

The liver is enlarged and there are innumerable low density lesions throughout
both lobes of the liver which are replacing the vast majority of the hepatic
parenchyma.  The portal vein is patent.  No intra or extrahepatic duct
dilatation.  The gallbladder is unremarkable.  There is a small amount of
ascites adjacent to the right lobe of the liver.  

The right kidney is mildly compressed anteriorly due to the enlarged liver. 
There is a subcentimeter low density lesion in the upper pole of the right
kidney, likely representing a subcentimeter cyst (sequence 5 image 31).  The
right kidney is otherwise unremarkable.  The left kidney is within normal
limits.  The adrenals and spleen are unremarkable.  The pancreas is within
normal limits. 

The small and large bowel are unremarkable.  No retroperitoneal or mesenteric
adenopathy. 

PELVIS: 

The images of the pelvis are substantially degraded secondary to the patient's
body habitus.  No pelvic masses are identified.  The bladder is unremarkable. 
The patient is status post supracervical hysterectomy.  No pelvic adenopathy. 
No free fluid within the pelvis. 

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES: 

Bilateral symmetrical sclerosis is identified on the iliac side of the
sacroiliac joints, consistent with osteitis condensans ilii.  There is severe
degenerative change within the right hip joint with joint space loss and
osteophyte formation.  No destructive osseous lesions.  

IMPRESSION:

Multiple pulmonary and hepatic metastases.  No separate primary lesion
identified.

The images of the pelvis are substantially degraded secondary to the patient's
body habitus.  No pelvic masses are identified.  The bladder is unremarkable. 
The patient is status post supracervical hysterectomy.  No pelvic adenopathy. 
No free fluid within the pelvis. 

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES: 

Bilateral symmetrical sclerosis is identified on the iliac side of the
sacroiliac joints, consistent with osteitis condensans ilii.  There is severe
degenerative change within the right hip joint with joint space loss and
osteophyte formation.  No destructive osseous lesions.
sections.impression:
Multiple pulmonary and hepatic metastases.  No separate primary lesion
identified.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  Shortness of breath, evaluate for pulmonary embolism.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT images were obtained through the chest following
administration of IV contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformations are
performed.  Right and left MIP reconstructions were performed.

COMPARISON:  CT torso on ___.

FINDINGS:

No axillary lymphadenopathy.  An enlarged subcarinal lymph node measuring 1.6
cm is unchanged.  Heart size is normal.  The aorta is normal in caliber. 
There is no central or segmental pulmonary artery filling defect.  Suboptimal
bolus limits evaluation of the subsegmental branches. No evidence of right
heart strain. No pericardial effusion. The airways are patent to the
subsegmental level. There are innumerable pulmonary nodules bilaterally,
similar in appearance to prior study in most cases although some have mildly
increased.  The largest measures 10 mm in diameter within the right lower
lobe.  No focal consolidation, pleural effusion or pneumothorax. 
Calcification along the right lateral lung is unchanged and consistent with
previous right lung surgery.  

The liver is very enlarged with multiple low-density lesions, similar to prior
study.  The exact degree of metastatic involvement is difficult to compare for
small changes but also seems to have increased.

The vertebral heights are preserved.  No acute bone abnormality.

IMPRESSION:

1.  No central or segmental filling defect in the pulmonary arteries.
Evaluation is slightly limited due to suboptimal IV bolus. 

2.  Innumerable bilateral pulmonary nodules, simas seen on the prior CT study
on ___, slightly increased.  No focal consolidation or pleural
effusion.  

3.  Enlarged liver with multiple hypodense lesions, with suggestion of
increased burden of disease.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  Squamous cell cancer mass.  Question mass, operative planning.

COMPARISON:  Prior outside chest CT from ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Volumetric, multi detector CT of the chest was performed with
intravenous contrast.  Images are presented for display in axial plane in 5 mm
and 1.25 mm collimation.  A series of multiplanar reformation images are also
submitted for review.  

Total exam DLP:  797 mGy-cm

FINDINGS:

CT OF THE THORAX:  There is an ill-defined right perihilar mass measuring
approximately 4.1 x 3.4 cm (02:14; 601b:38) extending along the major fissure
and into the thoracic inlet, adjacent to the brachiocephalic vessels.  The
mass is causing effacement and narrowing of the right main stem bronchus. 
Surrounding fibrotic changes of the right lung could reflect changes secondary
to radiation therapy. There is a 2.1 x 1.8 x 2.4 cm left hilar nodular mass
(2:23; 601b:40). There is a moderate-sized right-sided pleural effusion with
associated compressive atelectasis.  There is a stable 4 mm nodule in the
right upper lobe and note is made of a new patch opacity at the right lung
base. The left lung is essentially clear.  

A tracheostomy tube is seen in adequate position.  The airways are otherwise
patent.  There is no mediastinal or axillary lymph node enlargement by CT size
criteria.  The heart, pericardium and great vessels are otherwise within
normal limits.  

Although this study is not designed for assessment of intra-abdominal
structures, the visualized solid organs and stomach are unremarkable.  

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  No focal osseous lesion concerning for malignancy.  

IMPRESSION:

1. 4.1 cm right perihilar mass, causing effacement and narrowing of the right
mainstem bronchus and extending along the major fissure and into the thoracic
inlet, adjacent to the brachiocephalic vessels. 

2. 2.4 cm left hilar nodular mass.

3. Stable 4 mm nodule in the right upper lobe and new patchy opacity at the
right lung base.

4. Improved moderate sized right sided pleural effusion. 

Findings discussed with ___ by ___ via telephone on
___ 10:20 AM.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with lab findings concerning for hemolysis,
peripheral smear with atypical cells concerning for lymphoid/lymphoma.//
?lymphadenopathy

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent and reconstructed as
5 and 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm MIP
axial images. All images were reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Stationary Acquisition 0.5 s, 1.0 cm; CTDIvol = 1.2 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.2
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 4.0 s, 1.0 cm; CTDIvol = 9.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 9.3
mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 17.7 s, 64.3 cm; CTDIvol = 21.3 mGy (Body) DLP =
1,333.3 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,362 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

COMPARISON:  Multiple prior chest CTs, most recently ___.

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE AND CHEST WALL:
Status post left partial thyroidectomy.  No enlarged lymph nodes in either
axilla or thoracic inlet.  No abnormalities on the chest wall.  No
atherosclerotic calcifications in the head and neck arteries.

HEART AND VASCULATURE:
The heart is normal in size and shape.  No pericardial effusion.  No
significant atherosclerotic calcifications in the coronary arteries, aorta or
cardiac valves.  The pulmonary arteries and aorta are normal in caliber
throughout.

MEDIASTINUM AND HILA:
The esophagus is unremarkable.  Small mediastinal lymph nodes, none
pathologically enlarged by CT size criteria.  No hilar lymphadenopathy. 
Single small right diaphragmatic lymph node measuring up to 0.5 cm (4:191) in
short axis diameter.

PLEURA:
No pleural effusions.  No apical scarring bilaterally.

LUNGS:
The airways are patent to the subsegmental levels.  No bronchial wall
thickening, bronchiectasis or mucus plugging.  Small calcified granuloma in
the right lower lobe (04:34).  Unchanged small nodules in the right lower lobe
measuring 4 and 5 mm (4: 139 and 141 respectively) unchanged since ___.  Unchanged  3 mm nodule in the left upper lobe (4:89) also since
___.  No new nodules.

CHEST CAGE:
No acute fractures.  No suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesions.  S shaped
scoliosis of the thoracolumbar spine.

UPPER ABDOMEN:Please refer to same day abdominal CT report for
subdiaphragmatic findings.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  No lymphadenopathy in the chest.
2.  Pulmonary nodules measuring up to 5 mm, which are unchanged since
___ and likely benign.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with high grade lymphoma  // Follow up
assessment of disease.  Compare to prior studies.

TECHNIQUE:  ___ MD CT images were obtained through the chest after the
administration of IV contrast.  Multiplanar reformatted images in coronal and
sagittal axis were generated and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Stationary Acquisition 5.0 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 24.1 mGy (Body) DLP =
12.0 mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 8.5 s, 65.5 cm; CTDIvol = 28.5 mGy (Body) DLP =
1,867.6 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,880 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

COMPARISON:  CT chest from ___ and PET-CT from ___

FINDINGS: 

A hypodense right thyroid nodule is seen measuring 19 x 17 mm (3; 14).  There
is no supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy.  The
heart size is normal.  There is no pericardial effusion. The aorta is normal
in caliber.  The main pulmonary artery is normal in caliber.  The esophagus is
normal without evidence of wall thickening or a hiatal hernia.

For evaluation of the subdiaphragmatic structures, please refer to the
dedicated CT of the abdomen performed on the same day.

Osseous structures: No concerning focal lytic or sclerotic lesions are
identified.

4 mm right lower lobe nodule (3; 124), is unchanged compared to the prior
exam.
-a 3 mm right lower lobe nodule (3; 126), is unchanged compared to the prior
exam.  No concerning new or growing pulmonary nodules are identified.  There
is no pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

Subtle new lingular consolidation is seen.

IMPRESSION:


-No evidence of lymphadenopathy.
-Stable millimetric right-sided lung nodules without evidence of concerning
new or growing pulmonary nodules.
-19 mm right thyroid nodule, for which thyroid ultrasound may be helpful for
further characterization.
-Subtle new lingular consolidation could be infectious/inflammatory in
etiology.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.  Reformatted
coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal intensity
projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Stationary Acquisition 0.5 s, 1.0 cm; CTDIvol = 1.2 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.2
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 5.5 s, 1.0 cm; CTDIvol = 12.7 mGy (Body) DLP =
12.7 mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 19.2 s, 73.9 cm; CTDIvol = 10.8 mGy (Body) DLP =
777.1 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 811 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

FINDINGS: 

Lungs:

Parenchyma and Airways: Endotracheal tube tip is 4 cm above carina.  There are
mild tracheal, right mainstem bronchus secretions.  There is mild mucous
plugging in the left lower lobe.  Bilateral lower lobe opacities, likely
represent atelectasis.  There is mild interstitial edema.  There are changes
of paraseptal and moderately severe centrilobular emphysema in the lungs. 
There multiple benign calcified granuloma, more spur the prominent in the
right upper lung, with mild volume loss is, scarring.  Prominent subpleural
fat bilateral lung apices.  Few small lung nodules bilaterally, largest
measures 0.4 cm in the left mid lung series 6, image 134.
Vessels: Dilated main pulmonary artery, consistent with pulmonary artery
hypertension.  Central pulmonary arteries are patent.  Atherosclerotic changes
of normal caliber aorta.

Mediastinum and Hila: There few subcentimeter mediastinal lymph nodes, likely
reactive.  No hilar adenopathy.

Heart and Pericardium: Heart is enlarged.  Suggestion of chronic
subendocardial infarct.  Coronary artery calcifications.  Cardiac pacemaker
with lead tip in the right ventricle.

Pleura: Trace bilateral pleural effusions.

Neck, Thoracic Inlet, Axillae, Chest Wall: No mass, no adenopathy.  0.7 cm
right thyroid lobe nodule, of doubtful significance, no further follow-up is
indicated according to guidelines.

Upper Abdomen: See separately dictated report CT abdomen pelvis from today.

Chest Cage: There few chronic rib fractures.  Few small benign bone islands. 
No acute fractures.

IMPRESSION: 

Bilateral lower lobe opacities, most likely atelectasis, adjacent small
pleural effusions.  Component of pneumonia is unlikely, though it cannot be
excluded particularly on the right.  There is mucous plugging in the left
base, and trachea, right mainstem bronchus secretions.
Paraseptal and centrilobular emphysema.  Pulmonary artery hypertension.
Small lung nodules, largest 0.4 cm.  Follow-up chest CT without contrast in ___
year recommended

RECOMMENDATION(S):  Chest CT without contrast in ___ year

radiology_facts.report_text:
TYPE OF THE EXAM:  CT angiogram of the aorta.

REASON FOR THE EXAM AND MEDICAL HISTORY:  ___ gentleman with abdominal
aortic aneurysm.

COMPARISON EXAMS:  CT of the abdomen dated ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Multiple helical images through the chest, abdomen, and pelvis
were obtained initially without administration of intravenous contrast. Then
multiple helical images through the abdomen, chest, and pelvis were obtained
post-administration of intravenous contrast in the arterial phase as per
pre-stent evaluation protocol.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:  The thyroid gland is normal in appearance.  There is no axillary,
supraclavicular, or mediastinal lymphadenopathy.  The thoracic aorta
demonstrates multiple atherosclerotic calcifications.  There is no pericardial
effusion.  There is no pleural effusion.  There is no pneumothorax. 
Evaluation of the lung parenchyma demonstrates hypoventilatory changes with
consolidation within the lingula.

ABDOMEN:

Two small hypodense lesions within the liver are seen, unchanged from the
prior study.  There is a 5-mm arterially hyperenhancing lesion within the
liver dome, likely representing a small hemangioma or a vascular shunt.  There
is no intrahepatic biliary dilatation.  The patient is status post
cholecystectomy.  The spleen is normal in size with no evidence of focal
nodules.  Bilateral adrenal glands are normal with no focal nodules.  There
are bilateral renal cysts without evidence of suspicious masses or
hydronephrosis.  There is no nephrolithiasis.  Within the pancreas, there are
multiple coarse calcifications at the level of the tail and more prominent at
the level of the head which are associated with multiple small cystic lesions.
These likely represent cystic tumors, the largest one measures approximately
1.5 cm (4:120).  There is no pancreatic duct dilatation.  The common bile duct
is normal in size post-cholecystectomy.  The visualized small loops of bowel
within the upper abdomen demonstrate no abnormal dilatation.  There is no
lymphadenopathy.  There is no ascites.

Pelvis: Prostate gland is enlarged.  The urinary bladder is well distended
with no evidence of masses.  Colon is redundant with scattered diverticulae,
however, with no evidence of abnormal dilatation or masses.  There is no
ascites within the pelvis.  There is no lymphadenopathy.

CTA:  There is scattered atherosclerotic calcification along the abdominal
aorta.  The celiac axis vessels are patent.  The splenic artery, left gastric
and common hepatic artery are well opacified without evidence of significant
stenosis.  ___ demonstrates normal caliber.  Bilateral renal arteries are
normal in caliber with minimal plaques along the ostium of the left renal
artery.  There is an infrarenal abdominal aortic aneurysm, measuring 3.7 x 3.7
cm with presence of peripheral calcified and non-calcified plaque.  This
aneurysm extends approximately 6 cm in the craniocaudal dimension.  Bilateral
iliac arteries demonstrate minimal atherosclerosis, however, with no evidence
of anuerismal dilatation.  The external and internal iliac arteries
demonstrate normal caliber.  Common femoral arteries are normal bilaterally.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  There are post-traumatic changes at the level of pubic
symphysis and right sacrum.  There is diffuse spondylosis with no evidence of
destructive lytic lesions or acute fracture.

IMPRESSION:  3.7 x 3.7 infrarenal aortic aneurysm, which extends 6 cm
craniocaudally. Normal caliber of bilateral common iliac arteries.

Please refer to the 3D images for specific measurements, as per  pre-stent
placement protocol.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ female with worsening substernal chest pain and back
pain for four days with a pleuritic component.  Rule out PE or aortic
dissection.

COMPARISON:  Chest radiograph performed earlier the same day.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT-acquired axial images were obtained through the chest before
and after administration of 100 cc IV Optiray contrast.  Coronal and sagittal
reformats and oblique maximum intensity projection images are displayed.

CT PULMONARY ANGIOGRAM:  There is no acute pulmonary embolus.  The aorta is
normal without evidence of intramural hematoma, aneurysm or dissection.

There are at least two new lung nodules noted, the first in the right upper
lobe measuring 5 mm (2:28) and a 3-mm nodule in the left upper lobe (2:18). 
There is a third triangular-shaped density in the right lower lobe (2:39)
which could represent an intraparenchymal lymph node.  Scarring is noted at
the lung apices and peripherally in the right lung.  There is minimal
dependent subsegmental atelectasis.  No pleural effusion or pneumothorax. 
Airways are patent to the subsegmental level.  Heart is normal without
pericardial effusion.

The visualized portion of the liver, spleen, kidneys, and bilateral adrenal
glands are normal.

BONE WINDOWS:  No suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesion is identified.

IMPRESSION:

1.  No acute pulmonary embolus or aortic pathology.

2.  At least two pulmonary nodules measuring up to 5 mm.  Assuming no risk
factors for primary neoplasm, follow up suggested in 12 months.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ male with prostate cancer, rule out metastasis.

COMPARISON:  No prior.

TECHNIQUE:  64-row MDCT obtained of the chest, abdomen and pelvis with
scanning through the thoracic inlet to the proximal femora.  Coronal and
sagittal reformations reviewed alongside axial displays.

FINDINGS:

CT OF THE CHEST WITH IV AND ORAL CONTRAST:  There is a tiny left hypodense
thyroid nodule measuring 10 mm x 7 mm (3, 7).  There are subcentimeter
axillary lymph nodes and no mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes that meet CT
criteria for lymphadenopathy.  The heart and great vessels are unremarkable. 
This examination is not tailored to evaluate for pulmonary emboli; however,
there is no large central filling defect appreciated.  There is a small
calcifications seen at the aortic arch.  The lungs are clear with no pulmonary
nodule, pleural effusion, or focal consolidation.

CT ABDOMEN WITH IV AND ORAL CONTRAST:  There is no focal lesion seen in the
liver.  The liver enhances homogenously.  The portal vein is patent.  There is
no intra- or extra-hepatic biliary dilatation.  The adrenals, spleen and
gallbladder are unremarkable.  The esophagus and stomach are normal.  There
are bilateral exophytic renal hypodensities likely representing a benign renal
cyst.  The largest cyst on the left measures 2.7 x 2.4 cm (3, 72), and the
largest hypodensity on the right measures 3.0 x 2.3 cm (3, 69).  The pancreas
is normal.  There is no retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy by CT
criteria.  Intra-abdominal loops of large and small bowel are unremarkable.

CT PELVIS WITH IV AND ORAL CONTRAST:  Note is made of moderate diverticulosis
of the sigmoid colon without any evidence of diverticulitis.  Intrapelvic
loops of large and small bowel are unremarkable.  The bladder is distended
with urine without any signs of wall thickening.  There is one small 9-mm
right pelvic sidewall lymph node along the right external iliac chain in the
area of previous lymph node dissection (3, 109).  The patient is status post
prostatectomy with multiple surgical clips from side of the prostate.  There
is no evidence to suggest soft tissue recurrence in the surgical bed.  There
is no inguinal lymphadenopathy by CT criteria.  There is one mprominent right
external iliac lymph node measuring 9 mm in shortest dimension(3, 118). 
Otherwise, no lymph nodes actually meet CT criteria for lymphadenopathy.

BONE EVALUATION:  There are no lytic or sclerotic lesions to suggest
metastatic prostate disease.  There is no evidence of fracture.  There are no
significant degenerative changes.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No evidence to suggest overt metastatic disease.
2.  No evidence of recurrence in the prostatectomy bed.
3.  Prominent right pelvic sidewall lymph node and right external iliac lymph
node.

with urine without any signs of wall thickening.  There is one small 9-mm
right pelvic sidewall lymph node along the right external iliac chain in the
area of previous lymph node dissection (3, 109).  The patient is status post
prostatectomy with multiple surgical clips from side of the prostate.  There
is no evidence to suggest soft tissue recurrence in the surgical bed.  There
is no inguinal lymphadenopathy by CT criteria.  There is one mprominent right
external iliac lymph node measuring 9 mm in shortest dimension(3, 118). 
Otherwise, no lymph nodes actually meet CT criteria for lymphadenopathy.

BONE EVALUATION:  There are no lytic or sclerotic lesions to suggest
metastatic prostate disease.  There is no evidence of fracture.  There are no
significant degenerative changes.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial helical and the CT images were obtained of the chest,
abdomen and pelvis after the administration of IV and oral contrast. 
Additional sections through the abdomen were obtained 30 minutes post IV
contrast administration.  Multiplanar reformatted images were generated in the
coronal and sagittal planes.

DLP:  ___ mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT abdomen and pelvis, ___ CT torso, ___.

FINDINGS:

CT chest:  There is a 9 mm left thyroid nodule which is stable compared to
___.  The trachea is midline and the airways are patent to the
subsegmental level.  The lungs are clear without nodules, consolidations,
effusions or pneumothorax.

The heart pericardium and great vessels are unremarkable in appearance.  There
is no axillary, supraclavicular, hilar or mediastinal lymphadenopathy by CT
size criteria.

CT abdomen:  There is minimal focal fatty infiltration of segment 4B.  The
liver otherwise enhances homogeneously without focal lesions or biliary ductal
dilatation.  The portal vein is patent.  The gallbladder is unremarkable
without stones or wall thickening.  The spleen pancreas and adrenal glands are
unremarkable in appearance.  There is a 3.3 cm exophytic renal cyst in the
left upper pole as well as a 3.3 cm right upper pole exophytic renal cyst. 
There is an additional 8 mm round density in the left renal upper pole that is
too small to characterize with characterize by CT but likely represents a
cyst.  There is another 6 mm hypodensity in the right lower renal pole there
is a small with appearance most likely represents a cyst.  The kidneys
otherwise present symmetric nephrograms excretion of contrast without focal
solid lesions, all pelvocaliceal dilatation or perinephric abnormalities.

The stomach, duodenum and small bowel are unremarkable in appearance without
evidence of obstruction or focal wall thickening.  There is moderate colonic
fecal load as well as redundancy of the sigmoid colon in an otherwise
unremarkable large bowel.

The abdominal aorta is of normal caliber with patent celiac axis, SMA,
bilateral renal arteries and ___.  There has been interval resolution of
previously seen retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy with decrease in size and
number of previously seen nodes.  On today's examination there are no
pathologically enlarged mesenteric or retroperitoneal lymph nodes by CT size
criteria. There is no pneumoperitoneum or ascites noted.  There is a small
umbilical fat containing hernia.

CT pelvis:  The bladder is unremarkable in appearance.  There is mild rectal
wall thickening which is likely due to prior radiation therapy.  The prostate
is surgically absent with surgical clips in place.  There are no suspicious
abnormalities of the site of the previous prostate resection bed.  There is no
pelvic free fluid or free air.  A single prominent right external iliac lymph
node is unchanged in size.  There are no pathologically enlarged inguinal or
pelvic wall lymph nodes by CT size criteria.

Osseous structures: There are multilevel degenerative changes of the
thoracolumbar spine.  There is grade 1 anterolisthesis of L4 on L5.  There are
no focal blastic or lytic lesions in the visualized osseous structures
concerning for malignancy.


IMPRESSION:
1. No evidence of local or metastatic disease recurrence.  There has been
interval resolution of previously noted paraaortic/aortocaval lymphadenopathy
with decrease in size and number of previously appreciate nodes.

2. Stable 9 mm left thyroid nodule.

3. Bilateral renal cysts along with two other renal hypodensities that are too
small to fully characterize by CT but likely represent cysts.

HISTORY:  ___ male with history of prostate cancer, rising PSA, please
compare with prior 

COMPARISON:  Whole-body bone scan ___, CT abd pelvis ___ 

FINDINGS:

Chest:  Small hypodense left thyroid nodule measuring 7 mm unchanged.  If
clinical concern, thyroid ultrasound may be helpful in further evaluation. 
Mild calcific atherosclerosis of a normal caliber aortic arch and descending
aorta.  No evidence of significant mediastinal, hilar, or axillary
lymphadenopathy.  Visualized proximal airways appear patent.  Biapical
scarring.  Minimal bibasilar dependent subsegmental atelectasis.  

Abdomen:  Liver, gallbladder, spleen, pancreas, bilateral adrenal glands
appear unremarkable.  Bilateral simple appearing renal cortical cyst
unchanged.  Mild calcific atherosclerosis of a normal caliber abdominal aorta.
Slight interval increase in prominence of retroperitoneal lymph nodes
measuring up to 0.7 x 2.1 cm in short axis.  Visualized small bowel are
unremarkable.  No evidence of intraperitoneal free fluid. 

Pelvis:  Normal-appearing urinary bladder.  Evidence of prostatectomy.  No
evidence of pelvic free fluid.  No evidence of significant inguinal or pelvic
sidewall lymphadenopathy.  Stool throughout the colon.  Normal-appearing
appendix.  Visualized osseous structures are unremarkable.  

IMPRESSION:

1.  Slight interval increase in prominence of mildly enlarged retroperitoneal
lymph nodes.  

2.  Small hypodense left thyroid nodule.  If clinical concern, thyroid
ultrasound may be helpful in further evaluation.  

3.  Other chronic findings suggest bilateral simple renal cysts described
above.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  ___ male with history of prostate cancer on new treatment. 
Restaging.

COMPARISON:  CT torso from ___

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT-acquired axial images from the thoracic inlet to the pubic
symphysis were displayed with 5 mm slice thickness.  Oral and intravenous
contrast was administered.  Coronal and sagittal reformations were prepared.

CT CHEST WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:  An 8 mm nodule within the left thyroid
gland is unchanged from prior.  No supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal, or
hilar lymphadenopathy is identified.  The thoracic aorta is non-aneurysmal and
patent.  The heart size is normal, and there is no pericardial effusion. 
Pleural surfaces are clear without plaque or effusion.

The tracheobronchial tree is patent to subsegmental levels without bronchial
wall thickening or bronchiectasis.  Biapical scarring is unchanged.  A 4 mm
perifissural nodule within the right lung is unchanged from prior (2:45).  No
new suspicious pulmonary nodule is identified.

The liver demonstrates homogeneous parenchymal enhancement without suspicious
focal lesion.  Hepatic veins and portal venous system are grossly patent.  No
intra- or extra-hepatic biliary ductal dilatation is identified.  The
gallbladder, spleen, pancreas, and adrenal glands are normal.  There is
symmetric enhancement of the kidneys without suspicious focal lesion or
hydronephrosis.  Cysts within the upper poles of both kidneys are stable. 
Additional punctate hypodense lesions within the kidneys are too small to
characterize, though are unchanged and also likely represent additional cysts.
The abdominal aorta and branch vessels are non-aneurysmal and patent.  No
mesenteric lymphadenopathy is identified.  Previously seen scattered
retroperitoneal lymph nodes are unchanged and are not pathologically enlarged
by CT size criteria.  A dominant midline lymph node measuring 21 x 8 mm is
stable (2:92).  A representative left para-aortic lymph node is stable
measuring 7 mm (2:83).  Stomach and small bowel loops are normal in caliber
and configuration without evidence of obstruction or inflammation.  Appendix
is visualized and is normal.  There is no ascites.

CT PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:  The rectum and colon are normal in
caliber and configuration without evidence of obstruction or inflammation. 
The patient is status post prostatectomy and iliac lymphadenectomy.  No soft
tissue mass is identified within the resection bed.  Scattered pelvic lymph
nodes are stable and not enlarged by CT size criteria.  The bladder is
distended and appears normal.  

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES:  No bone destructive lesion or acute fracture is
identified.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Stable borderline retroperitoneal lymph nodes.  No new suspicious
lymphadenopathy within the chest, abdomen or pelvis.
2.  No evidence of distant metastatic disease.
3.  Stable subcentimeter left thyroid lesion.
4.  Unchanged 4 mm perifissural pulmonary nodule.

radiology_facts.report_text:
REASON FOR EXAMINATION:  Assessment of the patient with prostate cancer.

COMPARISON:  CT torso from ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT of the chest was obtained after administration of IV
contrast.  Axial images were reviewed in conjunction with coronal and sagittal
reformats.

FINDINGS:

Left thyroid nodule is redemonstrated, unchanged.  There is no evidence of
mediastinal, hilar, or axillary lymphadenopathy.  The aorta and pulmonary
arteries are normal in diameter.  Heart size is normal.  There is no
pericardial or pleural effusion.  The imaged portion of the upper abdomen
demonstrates left renal cyst, but the precise description of the abdomen would
be included in the separate report related to the CT abdomen.

There are no bone lesions worrisome for infection or neoplasm.

Airways are patent till the subsegmental level bilaterally.  Bilateral apical
scarring is unchanged.  Lungs are otherwise demonstrating right lower lobe and
left lower lobe nodules, 4:96, 190, 198, that are sub-3-mm and at least two of
them can be retrospectively seen on the prior CT examination.  No new nodules,
masses, or consolidations demonstrated.  Given the patient's history,
reassessment with chest CT in six months is recommended for assessment of
their stability.

IMPRESSION:

NO DEFINITIVE EVIDENCE OF METASTATIC DISEASE. SEVERAL PULMONARY NODULES THAT
SHOULD BE RE-ASSESSED IN 6 MONTHS FOR STABILITY

reconstructions.

axillary lymphadenopathy.  No enlarged lymph nodes in the mediastinum or at
the level of the hilar structures.  Stable appearance of the breasts.  Stable
mild coronary calcifications, no relevant valvular calcifications, no
pericardial effusion.  The anastomosis of the liver transplant is of identical
morphology.  There is a minimal new right pleural effusion.  No osteolytic
lesions at the level of the ribs, the sternum, or the vertebral bodies.  Mild
degenerative vertebral disease.  No vertebral compression fractures.
No suspicious pulmonary nodules or masses.  No diffuse lung disease.  No
pleural thickening.  The airways are patent.

hemodynamic compromise.  Coronary artery calcifications are mild.  The
thoracic aorta is normal in caliber.  Thoracic aorta and great vessel origin
calcifications are mild.  The main pulmonary artery is normal in caliber.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary or mediastinal lymphadenopathy is
present.  Indistinct soft tissue in the anterior mediastinum is unchanged
probably reflects reactive thyroid hyperplasia.

PLEURAL SPACES: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.  There is
mild linear left base atelectasis or scarring.  No pulmonary consolidation or
concerning pulmonary nodule.

BASE OF NECK: A 1.7 cm hypoattenuating thyroid nodule at the level of the
thyroid isthmus is unchanged since ___.

ABDOMEN: A similar-appearing wedge-shaped area of hypoattenuation is seen in
the right hepatic lobe, with subtle decrease of central density, corresponding
to right hepatic lobe radiofrequency ablation site.  The liver is slightly
nodular, compatible with provided history of cirrhosis.  Small accessory
spleen.  Varices are identified.  Surgical clips are noted in the upper
abdomen.  This is better characterized on the same-day dedicated abdominal MR.

___: No suspicious osseous abnormality is seen.? There is no acute fracture.


IMPRESSION:


1. No evidence of intrathoracic malignancy.
2. Increased, small pericardial effusion without evidence of hemodynamic
compromise.
3. Suboptimal evaluation of the upper abdomen, but minimally changed
appearance of the right hepatic lobe radiofrequency ablation site.  Cirrhotic
liver morphology.  This is better characterized on the concurrent dedicated MR
examination.
4. A 1.7 cm hypoattenuating thyroid nodule is unchanged since ___.  If
not previously performed, dedicated thyroid ultrasound should be considered
per ACR guidelines on incidentally discovered thyroid nodules.

___. If not previously performed, dedicated thyroid ultrasound should be
considered per ACR guidelines on incidentally discovered thyroid nodules.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with HCV cirrhosis of the liver and HCC and PVT
and a hx of RCC.  Evaluate for metastatic disease.

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector axial CT images were obtained through the chest
without intravenous contrast.  Multiplanar reformats including coronal,
sagittal, axial thin slice and axial maximum intensity projection images were
produced and uploaded to PACS.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 4.4 s, 34.5 cm; CTDIvol = 22.9 mGy (Body) DLP = 789.9
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 790 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Prior chest CT from ___

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: The thyroid gland is heterogeneous
with a 1.7 cm nodule at the isthmus.  No enlarged supraclavicular or axillary
lymph nodes.

UPPER ABDOMEN: The study is not optimized for the evaluation of
subdiaphragmatic structures.  Within this limitation, cirrhotic liver
morphology is present.  Enlarged spleen is partially imaged.  Hypodensity in
hepatic segment VII corresponds to the patient's known lesion.  A fiducial
marker is present in the left hepatic lobe. There is a small hiatus hernia.
Esophagus is patulous without evidence of obstruction.  Complete abdominal
findings will be reported on separately dictated abdominal MRI from the same
date.

MEDIASTINUM: No enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes. Air is present in the
esophagus without evidence of a mass or stricture.

HILA: No enlarged hilar lymph nodes

HEART and PERICARDIUM: Mild cardiomegaly has increased since ___. 
Coronary artery calcifications are mild.  Pericardium is physiologic.
PLEURA: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.
LUNG:

-PARENCHYMA:  Interlobular septal thickening and ground-glass appearance at
the lung bases is suggestive of mild dependent interstitial edema.  No focal
consolidation, mass or nodule suspicious for malignancy.
-AIRWAYS:  Airways are patent to the subsegmental level.
-VESSELS: The pulmonary veins are engorged, suggestive of pulmonary vascular
congestion.  The main and right pulmonary arteries are top normal in caliber. 
The left pulmonary artery is mildly dilated. Thoracic aorta is normal in
caliber with mild calcified atherosclerotic plaque.
CHEST CAGE: No suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesion or acute fracture.

IMPRESSION:


1. No evidence of metastatic disease in the chest.
2. Cardiomegaly has increased in comparison with ___.  This is
incompletely assessed on this study.  Recommend correlation with
echocardiography and clinical examination.
3. Pulmonary vascular congestion and mild dependent interstitial pulmonary
edema.
4. 1.7 cm thyroid nodule for which a non-urgent thyroid ultrasound is
recommended.
5. Abdomen is incompletely assessed on this study.  Lesion in the right
hepatic lobe is grossly unchanged and a fiducial marker is present in the left
hepatic lobe.  Please see the separately dictated liver MRI from the same date
for description of abdominal findings.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  Nonurgent thyroid ultrasound.
Correlation of increasing cardiomegaly with echocardiography and clinical
examination.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with HCV cirrhosis of the liver and HCC and PVT
and a hx of RCC// chest CT to r/o mets. PLEASE DO NOT GIVE IV CONTRAST

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector images of chest were obtained after the
administration of intravenous contrast with coronal, sagittal, axial maximum
intensity projection reformats.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 2.4 s, 37.7 cm; CTDIvol = 19.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 726.7
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 727 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CTs chest dated ___ and ___

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: There is a heterogeneous
hypoattenuated nodule in the isthmus of the thyroid measuring up to 1.7 cm
(2:7) stable dating back to CT chest ___.  There is no axillary or
supraclavicular lymphadenopathy.

UPPER ABDOMEN: There is a cirrhotic morphology liver.  Ill-defined hypodensity
in segment VII of the liver (02:50) corresponds to patient's known lesion. 
Fiducial marker is noted in the left lobe of the thyroid.  There is a small
hiatal hernia.

MEDIASTINUM: There is no mediastinal lymphadenopathy.

HILA: Within the limitations of a study without intravenous contrast, there is
no hilar lymphadenopathy.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: The heart is not enlarged.  There are minimal
calcifications in the coronary arteries.  There is no pericardial effusion.
PLEURA: There is no pleural effusion.
LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA:  There is no discrete nodule or consolidation.
2. AIRWAYS:  There is a patent to the subsegmental levels.
3. VESSELS:  The main pulmonary artery is borderline enlarged in size
measuring up to 2.9 cm (302:110)
CHEST CAGE: There is no acute osseous abnormality.

IMPRESSION:


1. No evidence of metastatic disease in the thorax.
2. Stable thyroid nodule.
3. Abdomen is incompletely evaluated on this study.  Within these limitations,
there is a cirrhotic morphology liver and ill-defined hypodensity in segment
VII of the liver that corresponds to patient's known lesion.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ female with diffuse bilateral infiltrates on outside
hospital chest x-ray and hemoptysis.  Assess for pulmonary masses or other
cause for hemoptysis.

COMPARISON:  Chest radiograph from ___ and from ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axial images of the chest were obtained with intravenous
contrast.  Coronal, sagittal and axial MIP reformations were prepared.

CT CHEST WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:  The thyroid gland is homogeneous without
focal nodule.  No supraclavicular or axillary adenopathy is identified.  There
are bulky mediastinal lymph nodes; a representative precarinal node measures
up to 13 mm in short axis diameter.  Subcarinal adenopathy measuring up to 16
mm (2:32) is noted.  There are bulky bilateral hilar lymph nodes as well.  The
thoracic aorta is non-aneurysmal and demonstrates no signs of acute aortic
syndrome.  Central pulmonary arteries are patent.  The heart size is normal,
and there is no pericardial effusion.

The tracheobronchial tree is patent to subsegmental levels without bronchial
wall thickening or bronchiectasis.  There are multiple areas of consolidation
throughout both lungs, most severe in the left upper lobe, lingula, left lower
lobe, and right lower lobe.  The right upper and middle lobes are relatively
less involved.  The consolidation has mixed solid and ground-glass attenuation
and follows a peribronchovascular distribution.  Areas of septal thickening
are also evident.  There is no pleural effusion.  Findings most likely reflect
multifocal pneumonia with reactive adenopathy, however, diffuse alveolar
hemorrhage is possible in the appropriate clinical setting.  A focal 11 x 9 mm
nodule is identified arising from the medial aspect of the minor fissure
(2:36).  There is no pleural effusion.  

Limited views of the upper abdominal viscera appear within normal limits.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Diffuse solid and ground-glass consolidations predominantly involving the
left upper and lower lobes and right lower lobe with peribronchovascular
distribution.  Findings are concerning for multifocal pneumonia with
associated reactive adenopathy, though diffuse alveolar hemorrhage is possible
in the appropriate clinical setting.  Neoplastic process is also not excluded.

2.  11 mm parafissural pulmonary nodule of uncertain etiology.  


Would recommend followup CT examination after acute symptoms have resolved (3
months or sooner) to ensure resolution of opacities and focal nodule.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ with Microscopic Polyangitis, presenting with small volume
hemoptysis, evaluate for diffuse alveolar hemorrhage.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was performed without
intravenous contrast agent reconstructed as contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick
axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  Total DLP (Body) = 237 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Chest radiographs dated ___.

FINDINGS: 

The thyroid is normal. Supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal and hilar lymph
nodes are not enlarged. Aorta and pulmonary arteries are normal size. Cardiac
configuration is normal and there is no appreciable coronary calcification. 
There is a trace pericardial effusion.

Diffuse scattered areas of ground-glass opacity in a peribronchovascular
distribution could represent inflammatory disorder, an infectious process, or
diffuse alveolar hemorrhage, depending on the clinical setting.  This process
is more confluent in the medial aspect of the right upper and middle lobes
(601b:  46, 35).  More focal nodular areas in bilateral lungs (02:17, 21, 28,
30, 49, 55), are likely more confluent areas of the same process.  There is
associated diffuse cylindrical bronchiectasis.  Short interval followup chest
CT after resolution of the acute process is recommended to exclude underlying
malignancy.  The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.

There is moderate dextroscoliosis of the thoracic spine.  There is no
worrisome osseous lesion.  The partially visualized upper abdomen is
unremarkable.  There is a small hiatal hernia.

IMPRESSION:


1. Diffuse areas of ground-glass opacity in a peribronchovascular distribution
could represent an inflammatory disorder, an infectious process, or diffuse
alveolar hemorrhage, depending on the clinical setting.
2. Several more nodular opacities are seen bilaterally which may represent
extension of the underlying process described above, however short interval
follow-up chest CT after symptoms have resolved is recommended to ensure
resolution.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA chest

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with hypoxia, tachypnea, tachycardia.// Please
eval for PE, eval for bronchiectasis per CTA H/N recs.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.
Reformatted coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal
intensity projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 3.0 s, 39.7 cm; CTDIvol = 4.5 mGy (Body) DLP = 180.1
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 0.6 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 1.5 mGy (Body) DLP = 0.8
mGy-cm.
   3) Stationary Acquisition 2.4 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 6.2 mGy (Body) DLP = 3.1
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 184 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CTA head and neck dated ___, CT chest dated ___.

FINDINGS: 

The aorta and its major branch vessels are patent, with no evidence of
stenosis, occlusion, dissection, or aneurysmal formation.  There is no
evidence of penetrating atherosclerotic ulcer or aortic arch atheroma present.

The pulmonary arteries are well opacified to the subsegmental level, with no
evidence of filling defect within the main, right, left, lobar, segmental or
subsegmental pulmonary arteries.  The main and right pulmonary arteries are
normal in caliber, and there is no evidence of right heart strain.  The heart
is not enlarged.  There is a small pericardial effusion.

There is no supraclavicular, axillary, or hilar lymphadenopathy.  Multiple
prominent although not technically enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes are
present, likely reactive.  The thyroid gland appears unremarkable.

There is no pneumothorax or pleural effusion.

Airways are patent centrally.  Centrilobular and paraseptal emphysematous
changes are noted bilaterally.  Biapical pleuroparenchymal scarring is present
there is diffuse cylindrical bronchiectasis and bronchial wall thickening,
which has progressed as compared to ___, and is most notable in the bilateral
upper lobes.  Extensive ___, nodular and ground-glass opacities are
seen throughout all pulmonary lobes, new from ___. The largest pulmonary
nodule is located in the right upper lobe, and measures 11 mm in mean diameter
(301:64).  Subpleural reticulation is seen at the bilateral lung bases. 
Mucous plugging is noted within small airways.  Focal atelectasis versus
scarring is seen involving a portion of the right lower lobe (602:29).

Limited images of the upper abdomen are notable for a small hiatal hernia. 
Incidental note is made of a small accessory spleen.  There is diffuse
thickening of the left adrenal gland, without focal nodule identified.

No lytic or blastic osseous lesion suspicious for malignancy is identified. 
Again noted is moderate dextroscoliosis of the thoracic spine.

IMPRESSION:


1. No evidence of pulmonary embolism or acute aortic abnormality.
2. Nonspecific diffuse multifocal ___, nodular, and ground glass
opacities favor an infectious or inflammatory process.  The ground-glass
component is nonspecific, though could be partially explained by mild diffuse
alveolar hemorrhage, given the patient's history.
3. Diffuse cylindrical bronchiectasis and bronchial wall thickening has
progressed since ___.
4. Small pericardial effusion.
5. Pulmonary nodules, measuring up to 11 mm in mean diameter.
 For incidentally detected multiple solid pulmonary nodules bigger than 8mm, a
CT follow-up in 3 to 6 months is recommended in a low-risk patient, with an
optional CT follow-up in 18 to 24 months. In a high-risk patient, both a CT
follow-up in 3 to 6 months and in 18 to 24 months is recommended.

See the ___ ___ Guidelines for the Management of Pulmonary
Nodules Incidentally Detected on CT" for comments and reference:
___

mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 184 mGy-cm.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with pancreatic adenocarcinoma// r/o mets

TECHNIQUE:   Multi-detector helical scanning of the chest, coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic, iodinated contrast agent, following oral
administration of contrast agent for selected abdominal studies, and/or
followed by scanning of the neck, was reconstructed as contiguous 5 mm and 1.0
or 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 or 5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm
MIP axial images. Concurrent scanning of the abdomen and pelvis and/or neck
will be reported separately. All images of the chest were reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 4.9 s, 31.9 cm; CTDIvol = 6.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 197.0
mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 11.2 s, 72.7 cm; CTDIvol = 6.4 mGy (Body) DLP = 460.6
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 658 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CTA
PANCREAS (ABDOMEN AND PELVIS))

COMPARISON:  Chest CT one ___ performed elsewhere.

FINDINGS: 

Supraclavicular and axillary lymph nodes are not enlarged and there are no
soft tissue abnormalities elsewhere in the chest wall concerning for
malignancy.  Findings below the diaphragm will be reported separately.

Cardio mediastinum:

Esophagus is mildly patulous, otherwise unremarkable.  There are no thyroid
findings warranting further imaging evaluation.  Atherosclerotic calcification
is mild in head and neck vessels, present in at least left main and anterior
descending coronary arteries.  Aorta and pulmonary arteries are normal size. 
Heart is not enlarged.  Pericardium is physiologic.

Thoracic lymph nodes:

None pathologically enlarged.

Lungs, airways, and pleura:

Bilateral apical pleuroparenchymal scarring, more nodular than usual, but
symmetric and unchanged since ___.

Mild scattered subpleural ground-glass and interstitial thickening, posterior
right lung base is probably post inflammatory, has changed slightly in
distribution, most often seen with chronic aspiration.  There is no pneumonia
or bronchiectasis.

Well-circumscribed 6 x 4 mm soft tissue nodular opacity, left lower lobe,
4:228, unchanged since ___.  8 mm long linear lesion, anterior left
lung base, 4:237, and a triangular region of subpleural atelectasis in the
lingula surrounding minimally dilated peripheral bronchus, 4:228, also
unchanged.  No new or enlarging lung nodules or other focal lesions of
consequence.

Chest cage:

 Although there are no bone lesions in the imaged chest cage suspicious for
malignancy or infection, it should be noted that radionuclide bone and FDG PET
scanning are more sensitive in detecting early osseous pathology than chest CT
scanning..,

IMPRESSION: 

No evidence of intrathoracic malignancy.  2 small left lung lesions stable
since ___ can be considered benign.

Coronary atherosclerosis.

Inflammatory alveolar and interstitial findings, right lung base, changed
minimally since ___ most commonly due to chronic aspiration.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST WITHOUT CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with 5 lbs. weight loss, cough, SOB, found to
have large R pleural effusion c/f malignancy// eval for mass or other etiology
for Large R pleural effusion s/p chest tube placement ad lymphadenopathy

TECHNIQUE:  CT of the chest was performed without administration of IV
contrast.  Coronal, sagittal and axial MIP reformat were performed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 2.3 s, 36.7 cm; CTDIvol = 15.9 mGy (Body) DLP = 582.0
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 582 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CTA chest ___

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: A left chest wall pacemaker remains
in place with its leads terminating in the right atrium and right ventricle.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Again noted is a calcified aneurysm of the splenic artery
measuring 1.7 x 1.5 cm (301:50).  Cholelithiasis is also present.  A partially
imaged left renal cyst is unchanged.  Two 5 mm hypodensity in the inferior
right hepatic lobe (301:61) and left hepatic lobe are nonspecific (301:49).

MEDIASTINUM: Multiple surgical clips are again noted in the superior
mediastinum.

HILA: There is no hilar adenopathy, although evaluation is limited by the
absence of intravenous contrast.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: There are postsurgical changes from CABG.  Severe
coronary artery calcifications are noted.  There is no pericardial effusion.

PLEURA: A right chest tube is in place. There is a small right pleural
effusion, which is significantly decreased in size compared to prior CT.  A
small right pneumothorax is also noted.

LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA: Diffuse ground-glass opacity throughout the right lung likely
represents edema in setting of recent re-expansion.  There are areas of more
dense consolidation in the right middle lobe and right lower lobe.  Mild
dependent atelectasis is appreciated in the left lung.  There are multiple
pulmonary nodules in right upper lobe measuring up to 4 mm (for example,
302:30, 67).  There is a punctate nodule in the left lung apex (___) and a
2 mm pulmonary nodule in the left upper lobe (302:47).
2. AIRWAYS:  The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.
3. VESSELS:  Evaluation of the vasculature is limited due to lack of IV
contrast.  There is mild atherosclerotic disease in the aorta.  There is no
aortic aneurysm.
CHEST CAGE: Median sternotomy wires are in place.  There is a defect in the
most inferior median sternotomy wire.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  Interval reexpansion of the right lung status post chest tube placement,
with scattered areas of ground glass opacity in the right lung likely
representing reexpansion pulmonary edema.
2.  A small right pneumothorax, which was not visualized on prior chest
radiographs, and a small residual right pleural effusion.
3.  Areas of residual opacity primarily in the right middle lobe and right
lower lobe are favored to represent atelectasis, however underlying pneumonia
or a small mass cannot be entirely excluded.
4.  Unchanged calcified aneurysm of the splenic artery.
5.  Nonspecific 5 mm hypodensities in the liver, which can be further
evaluated with MRI Abdomen with contrast.

radiology_facts.report_text:
REASON FOR EXAM:  Pulmonary nodules incidentally seen on prior shoulder CT.

COMPARISON:  Shoulder CT from ___.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT images of the chest with 5-mm and 1.25-mm axial
slices and coronal and sagittal reformats were submitted for interpretation. 
No IV contrast was administered.  

FINDINGS: The thyroid gland appears unremarkable.  Minimal soft tissue in the
anterior mediastinum likely represents residual thymus. There is no
pathologically enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy by size criteria,
evaluation is limited due to lack of IV contrast.  Heart is not enlarged. 
There is no pericardial effusion. Minimal atherosclerotic calcifications of
the aorta is noted.

Multiple pulmonary nodules are identified:  4-mm nodule in the left upper lobe
(4:49).  sub-mm bronchiolar-subpleural nodules in the right upper lobe (4:62).
1-mm inferior right upper lobe nodule (4:62).  3-mm lingular nodule (4:103). 
4-mm right lower lobe nodule with spiculation (4:117).  Ill-defined opacity at
the base of the lingula likely represents scarring. There is no pneumothorax,
pleural effusion, or focal pulmonary consolidation.  Airways are patent to the
level of subsegmental branches.  There is bilateral mild apical
thickening-scarring.  Subtle emphysema in bilateral upper lobes are mild.  

Upper abdominal organs are partially imaged and appear grossly unremarkable.

No osseous lesion to suggest malignancy or infection is identified.

IMPRESSION:
1. Multiple pulmonary nodules as described above, with the largest measuring 4
mm with spiculation in the right lower lobe.  Chest CT in ___ year is
recommended for further evaluation.
2.. Mild bi-apical emphysema.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ with shortness of breath and hypoxia.  Elevated D-
dimer.  Evaluate for PE.

No prior examinations.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was performed prior to
and following the administration of 90 mL of IV Optiray contrast using a non-
gated CTA protocol.  Coronal, sagittal, and oblique MIP reformats were
displayed.

CT OF THE CHEST:  This exam is limited due to respiratory motion. There is no
evidence of pulmonary embolism in the central or lobar pulmonary arteries. The
segmental branches also appear patent, though the subsegmental branches cannot
be evaluated.  The lung parenchyma is also difficult to evaluate due to
motion.  There is a 6-mm nodule in the left upper lobe (3:46). There is also a
granuloma in the right upper lobe (3:17).  A moderate-sized nonhemorrhagic
loculated right pleural effusion is present with associated atelectasis.  A
part of the atelectasis appears to be taking a rounded configuration
suggesting a relatively chronic process.  There is no enhancement of the
pleura or thickening to suggest an empyema.  There is no pneumonia.  The
bronchi are patent to the subsegmental level.

Heart size is normal and there is no pericardial effusion.  A left lower
paratracheal node measures 8 mm and right high paratracheal node measures 9
mm.  There is no pathologic mediastinal, hilar, or axillary lymph node
enlargement.

The visualized portions of the liver and spleen are unremarkable.  There is a
small axial hiatal hernia.

There are no suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesions, and no rib fracture.

IMPRESSION:

1.  Limited exam due to respiratory motion.  No evidence of pulmonary embolism
in the main or lobar pulmonary arteries.

2.  Moderate-sized nonhemorrhagic loculated right pleural effusion of unknown
etiology.  There is no evidence of empyema or pneumonia.  Pleural fluid
sampling may be of value.

3.  6 mm left upper lobe nodule - if the patient has no risk factors, a 12-
month follow-up Chest Ct is recommended.  If there are risk factors (smoking
or a history of malignancy), a followup in ___ months is recommended.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  ___ female with inflammatory, bloody right effusion, ___
x2 in ___, also left nodules.  Evaluate further for effusion, nodules.

COMPARISON:  Prior study on ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT of the chest was obtained after the administration of 75 cc
of intravenous contrast.  Images were reformatted in the axial, coronal, and
sagittal planes.

CT CHEST WITH CONTRAST:  No pleural effusion or pleural thickening on today's
study.  Previously described 4-mm lung nodule in the lingula (2:36) has
slightly decreased in size and a 2-mm pleural-based nodule in right lower lobe
(2:37) is unchanged since prior study. Remaining small nodular opacities in
lower lobes (right more than left). Interval improvement of right upper lung
ground glass opacities (4:53). Small mediastinal lymph nodes, but not
pathologically enlarged according to CT size criteria. Unchanged appearance of
6.5 mm right hilar lymph node (2:27). The heart is normal in size.  Minimal
calcifications in normal-sized aorta. Limited visualization of abdominal
organs is unremarkable except for thickened right adrenal gland which is
stable.

BONE WINDOWS:  Degenerative changes in spine.  No suspicious lesions in
osseous structures.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No pleural effusion or thickening on today's study.
2.  Slight decrease in size of the 4-mm lung nodule located in lingula and
stable size of a 2- mm lung nodule in RLL.
3.  Remaining nodular opacities in lower lobes (right more than left) requires
a 6 month follow up.
4.  Interval improvement of ground glass opacities in RUL.

radiology_facts.report_text:
COMPUTED TOMOGRAPHY OF THE THORAX 

Followup of pulmonary nodules, evaluation for stability.

COMPARISON:  ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Volumetric CT acquisitions over the entire thorax in inspiration,
no administration of intravenous contrast material, multiplanar
reconstructions.

FINDINGS:  The examination is compared to ___.  The pre-existing
lung nodules are unchanged in size and morphology.  The reference lesion,
which simultaneously is the largest nodule, is located in the lingula (3, 29)
and still measures 4 mm in diameter.  There is no evidence of newly occurred
lung nodules.  The pre-existing subtle ground-glass opacities in the posterior
aspects of the lingula are unchanged.  Otherwise, no diffuse or focal
parenchymal changes.  The pleural surfaces are even, there is no pleural
effusion.  Normal hilar and mediastinal lymph nodes, no visible cardiac
abnormalities.  No abnormalities seen in the abdomen.  

IMPRESSION:  
1.  No change of pulmonary nodules.  According to the ___ criteria,
these nodules do not require further CT followup.
2.  Unchanged lingular ground-glass opacity.
3.  No other lung abnormalities.
4.  No hilar or mediastinal adenopathy.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ woman with pleuritic chest pain, question PE or other
acute process.

COMPARISONS:  CT chest without contrast from ___, CT chest with
contrast from ___ and CTA chest with and without contrast from
___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axial imaging was obtained through the chest, both prior to
and after the administration of intravenous contrast material according to the
PE protocol.  Coronal, sagittal and oblique reformats were completed.

FINDINGS:  There is no pulmonary embolism.  The aorta is normal in course and
caliber without evidence of aneurysm or dissection.  Note is made of a bovine
aortic arch.  There is no mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy.  The heart and
pericardium are unremarkable.  There is no pericardial effusion.  The
preexisting lung nodules are unchanged in size and morphology and no new
nodules are seen.  The largest nodule which measures 4 mm in the lingula is
unchanged from prior study.   There discoid atelectasis in the left lower lobe
and  a small left pleural effusion.  No focal consolidation or masses are
seen.  The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.   Evaluation of the
subdiaphragmatic structures is limited; however, the visualized portion of the
liver, gallbladder, pancreas, spleen and kidneys are unremarkable.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  Mild degenerative changes noted in the thoracic spine. 
There is no suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesion.

IMPRESSION:

1.  No evidence of PE.

2.  Small left pleural effusion.

3.  No change of pulmonary nodules.  These nodules do not require further CT
followup.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  Endometrial cancer.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT-acquired 5 mm axial images of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis
were obtained following the uneventful administration of 150 cc of Omnipaque
intravenous contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformations were performed at 5
mm slice thickness.

Examination DLP: 1416 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT chest examinations available from ___ through ___.  

CHEST CT WITH IV CONTRAST:  
The thyroid appears normal.  There is no axillary, mediastinal, or hilar
lymphadenopathy.  The heart size is normal, and there is no pericardial
effusion.  The great vessels are patent and normal in caliber.  No large
pulmonary embolus is detected to the segmental levels.  Again seen is a focal
3.5 mm lingular nodule (3:33) which is unchanged since the ___ CT
chest examination.  A 3 mm right lower lobe posterior nodule (3:47) appears
new since the ___ chest CTA examination, but, given its location,
may represent transient focal atelectasis rather than a solid nodule.  There
is no pleural effusion.

ABDOMEN CT WITH IV CONTRAST:  
Mild diffuse hepatic steatosis is present (3: 52).  The gallbladder, spleen,
adrenal glands, kidneys, pancreas, stomach, and intrapelvic small large bowel
are within normal limits.  Within the interpolar region of the right kidney is
a 15 mm hypodense lesion, likely a simple cyst.  A subcentimeter hypodensity
within the interpolar region of left kidney (3:75) is also statistically
likely a cyst, but too small for further characterization. There is no
mesenteric or retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy, and no free air or free fluid. 
There is mild central misty mesentery (3:74).  

PELVIS CT WITH IV CONTRAST:  
Trace fluid resides within the endometrial cavity (413:46).  The uterus
otherwise appears normal.  There is no neighboring lymphadenopathy.  No
adnexal masses are detected.  There is no intrapelvic free fluid.  The rectum,
bladder, sigmoid colon, and intrapelvic small large bowel are within normal
limits.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  
There is no acute fracture.  There are no bony lesions suspicious for
malignancy or infection.  There is mild levoscoliosis centered about the upper
lumbar spine (43:45).

IMPRESSION:
1.   3 mm left lingular nodule appears unchanged since the earliest available
study from ___.
2.   3 mm right basilar nodule appears new the since the most recent CT
examination from ___.  Mildfocal atelectasis can be considered
given the location, however continued attention to this area is recommended,
and, per ___ guidelines, followup CT at 6 months is can be considered
to assess for stability or resolution. 
3. No intra-abdominal or intrapelvic metastasis.
4. Hepatic steatosis.
5. Mild central misty mesentery is non-specific. No dedicated followup is
required, however, attention to this region is recommended on future cross
sectional studies.

sections.technique:
MDCT-acquired 5 mm axial images of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis
were obtained following the uneventful administration of 150 cc of Omnipaque
intravenous contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformations were performed at 5
mm slice thickness.

Examination DLP: 1416 mGy-cm.

new since the ___ chest CTA examination, but, given its location,
may represent transient focal atelectasis rather than a solid nodule.  There

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  Prostate cancer.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent  reconstructed as
axial, coronal , parasagittal, and ,MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  DLP: Reported in the concurrent abdomen CT

COMPARISON:  None.

FINDINGS: 

The thyroid is normal. Supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal and hilar lymph
nodes are not enlarged. Aorta and pulmonary arteries are normal size. Cardiac
configuration is normal and there are mild coronary calcifications in the LAD.
Multiple less than 3 mm lung nodules are as follows (6: 100, 105, 106, 131,
138, 139, 145, 157, 160, 181, 192, 193, 196), there is a 6 mm nodule in the
right lower lobe (6, 211)
There is no pleural or pericardial effusion.
Please refer to the concurrent CT abdomen for complete description of the
intra-abdominal findings
There are no osseous blastic lesion.

IMPRESSION: 

Multiple unspecific lung nodules, largest in the right lower lobe, followup in
3 months is recommended.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with prior chest ct scan that showed nodules;
please compare to prior exam and assess stability as recommended by radiology 
// Interval change

TECHNIQUE:   Multidetector scanning of the chest was performed and
reconstructed as contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal
and sagittal, and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images. No IV Contrast administered.
DOSAGE:   Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 7.0 s, 37.2 cm; CTDIvol = 9.7 mGy (Body) DLP = 364.7
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 365 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT ___ dating back to ___.

FINDINGS: 

FINDINGS:

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: Thyroid is surgically absent. 
There is no axillary or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy.  The superficial soft
tissues are normal.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Subcentimeter hypodensities in the left lobe of the liver are
too small to characterize but likely represent simple hepatic cysts.A tiny
right adrenal nodule is consistent with an adenoma or myelolipoma. The abdomen
is otherwise normal.

MEDIASTINUM: There is no mediastinal lymphadenopathy.

HILA: There is no hilar lymphadenopathy.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: There is no pericardial effusion.  Aortic annular and
coronary calcifications are moderate.
PLEURA: There is no pleural abnormality or pleural effusion.
LUNG:

-PARENCHYMA:  Numerous pulmonary nodules in the right lower lobe (series 5,
image 139, 148, 160, 203, 222) measure up to 5 mm.  There is mild scarring at
the lung bases, bilaterally.  A pulmonary nodule at the left lung base (series
5, image 192) is unchanged.  A 3 mm pulmonary nodule in the superior segment
of the left lower lobe (series 5, image 128) is unchanged.  A 3 mm pulmonary
nodule in the left upper lobe (series 5, image 99) is unchanged.  There are no
new or enlarging pulmonary nodules.
-AIRWAYS:  Narrowing of the coronal diameter of the upper trachea is mild,
usually seen in patients with chronic lung disease.  The airways are patent to
the subsegmental level.  The aorta and main pulmonary artery are normal in
size.
CHEST CAGE: There are no concerning osseous lesions for malignancy or
infection.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  Multiple pulmonary nodules, unchanged from ___.
2.  Tiny right adrenal nodule is consistent with an adenoma or myelolipoma.
