radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:
  Patient with history of prostate cancer evaluate for metastasis

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector CT of the chest was performed after the
administration of intravenous contrast.  Axial coronal and sagittal
reconstructions were acquired.  Maximum intensity projections were also
acquired

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Sequenced Acquisition 0.5 s, 0.4 cm; CTDIvol = 5.7 mGy (Body) DLP = 2.3
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 32.8 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 560.0 mGy (Body) DLP =
112.0 mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 10.4 s, 67.4 cm; CTDIvol = 16.3 mGy (Body) DLP =
1,085.2 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,200 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

COMPARISON:
To prior study done on ___

FINDINGS: 

THORACIC INLET: The thyroid is unremarkable. There are no enlarged
supraclavicular lymph nodes.  Note is made of saber sheath appearance of the
trachea.

BREAST AND AXILLA  : There are no enlarged axillary lymph nodes.

MEDIASTINUM: There are no enlarged mediastinal hilar lymph nodes.  Heart size
is normal.  The aorta and pulmonary arteries are normal in caliber.  There is
moderate coronary artery calcification.  There is no pericardial effusion.


PLEURA: There is no pleural effusion

LUNG: All the previously visualized pulmonary nodules are unchanged in size
and density.  (6, 89, 131, 135, 193, 195) no new pulmonary nodules.  Minimal
bibasilar atelectasis

BONES AND CHEST WALL : Review of bones is unremarkable.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Limited sections through the upper abdomen shows mild bilateral
adrenal thickening.  Please refer to dedicated report on abdomen which has
been dictated separately.

IMPRESSION:



RECOMMENDATION(S):  The all the previously visualized pulmonary nodules are
unchanged in size and density and the range in size from 1-2 mm.  No new
pulmonary nodules.

Please refer to dedicated report on abdomen which has been dictated
separately.

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with ___ prostate cancer and new breast
discomfort.// evaluate for metastatic disease

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector CT of the chest was performed after the
administration of intravenous contrast.  Axial coronal and sagittal
reconstructions were acquired.  Maximum intensity projections were also
acquired

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Sequenced Acquisition 0.5 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 9.2 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.8
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 4.7 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 77.7 mGy (Body) DLP =
15.5 mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 12.7 s, 67.2 cm; CTDIvol = 23.6 mGy (Body) DLP =
1,591.8 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,609 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

COMPARISON:
To a prior study done on ___

FINDINGS: 

THORACIC INLET: The thyroid is absent.  There are no enlarged supraclavicular
lymph nodes

BREAST AND AXILLA  : There are no enlarged axillary lymph nodes.  Incidental
note is made of a right-sided gynecomastia

MEDIASTINUM: There are no enlarged mediastinal hilar lymph nodes.  Heart size
is normal.  There is no pericardial effusion.  The airways are patent


PLEURA: There is no pleural effusion

LUNG: All the previously visualized pulmonary nodules are unchanged in size
density and morphology (6, 97 could 132 162, 207, 198).  Minimal subsegmental
atelectasis in both lung bases.

BONES AND CHEST WALL : Review of bones shows degenerative changes involving
the thoracic spine.  Compression deformity involving L1 vertebral body.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Limited sections through the upper abdomen are unremarkable.

IMPRESSION: 

Stable 2-3 mm bilateral pulmonary nodules.  No new pulmonary nodules.

Minimal bibasilar atelectasis.  Please refer to dedicated report on abdomen
which has been dictated separately.

EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with prostate cancer.  Restaging to evaluate for
disease progression.// evaluate for disease progression

TECHNIQUE:  Volumetric CT acquisitions over the entire thorax in inspiration,
administration of intravenous contrast material, multiplanar reconstructions.

DOSE:  DLP: Given in abdominal CT report.

COMPARISON:  ___.

FINDINGS: 

No incidental thyroid findings.  No supraclavicular, infraclavicular or
axillary lymphadenopathy.  No enlarged lymph nodes in the mediastinum or at
the level of the hilar structures.  No abnormalities at the level of the large
mediastinal vessels.  No incidental pulmonary embolism.  Moderate coronary

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with redo stenotomy/ AVR  // eval for aortic
calcifications      eval for aortic calcifications

TECHNIQUE:  Volumetric, multidetector CT of the chest was performed without
intravenous or oral contrast administration.  Images are presented for display
in the axial plane at 5 mm and 1.25 mm collimation.  A series of multiplanar
reformation images are also submitted for review.

Total exam DLP: 631 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Same-day preoperative chest radiograph. Otherwise, no prior
studies are available for comparison.

FINDINGS: 

There is a 7 mm hypodense nodule in the left lobe of the thyroid gland (2:6).
A right lower paratracheal lymph node measures 19 x 12 mm (2:23). No
significant axillary, supraclavicular or hilar lymphadenopathy is detected.
The esophagus is unremarkable.

The patient is status post aortic valve replacement and homograft of the
ascending aortic root with heavy calcification of the valve and homograft.
Coronary calcification is mild in the proximal LAD and circumflex arteries.
The heart is mildly enlarged without pericardial effusion. Calcification of
the mitral valve is mild.

Approximately 1 cm above the valve, the ascending aorta measures 33 x 30 mm
(02:31). At the same level, the pulmonary artery measures 29 mm in diameter
and the descending thoracic aorta measures 29 x 28 mm. The ascending aorta
measures up to 41 mm in maximal diameter above the level of the aortic
homograft (2:26) with a typical 3 vessel takeoff from the arch.  There is no
substantial calcification of the ascending aorta above the level of the
homograft or in the aortic arch and descending thoracic aorta. The pulmonary
arterial trunk is normal in caliber, but the right pulmonary artery is dilated
measuring 34 mm in diameter.

There is a small to moderate right pleural effusion and small left pleural
effusion, which are not hemorrhagic. There is associated mild compressive
atelectasis of the bilateral lower lobes. There is focal scarring and
bronchiectasis in the medial right middle lobe. Peribronchovascular
ground-glass opacities in the superior segment of the right lower lobe and
apical segment of the right upper lobe do not have the typical smooth
appearance of pulmonary edema.  Paraseptal emphysema is mild in the right
upper lobe.

The patient is status post median sternotomy without evidence of
complications. No blastic or lytic lesion suspicious for malignancy is
present.  Multilevel degenerative changes are moderate in the mid to lower
thoracic spine.

Although this study is not tailored for the evaluation of subdiaphragmatic
contents, the imaged upper abdomen shows no acute abnormality. There is mild
vascular calcification of the upper abdominal aorta.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  Peribronchovascular ground-glass opacities in the right upper and lower
lobes are suspicious for
2.  Borderline enlarged right lower paratracheal lymph node is likely
reactive.
3.  Small to moderate right and small left pleural effusions.
4.  Dilation of the right pulmonary artery.

NOTIFICATION:   The findings were discussed by Dr. ___ with NP ___
of cardiac surgery via telephone on ___ at 4:36 ___, 5 minutes after
discovery of the findings.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  History: ___ with SOB, concern for pna vs PE and DVT in RLE u/s 
wt 460 lbs  // History: ___ with SOB, concern for pna vs PE and DVT in RLE u/s
wt 460 lbs

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.
Reformatted coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal
intensity projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Stationary Acquisition 6.0 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 57.8 mGy (Body) DLP =
28.9 mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 4.1 s, 32.1 cm; CTDIvol = 23.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 749.8
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 779 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  None

FINDINGS: 

The exam is limited by body habitus and respiratory motion.

The aorta and its major branch vessels are patent, with no evidence of
stenosis, occlusion, dissection, or aneurysmal formation.  There is no
evidence of penetrating atherosclerotic ulcer or aortic arch atheroma present.

The pulmonary arteries are well opacified to the segmental level, with no
evidence of filling defect within the main, right, left, lobar pulmonary
arteries.  Evaluation of the segmental and subsegmental pulmonary arteries is
limited by respiratory motion.  The main and right pulmonary arteries are
normal in caliber, and there is no evidence of right heart strain.

There are ir mildly enlarged left prevascular lymph nodes measuring up to 1.1
cm (02:34).  There is no supraclavicular, axillary, or hilar lymphadenopathy. 
The thyroid gland appears unremarkable.

There is no evidence of pericardial effusion.  There is no pleural effusion.

There is a 4 mm left upper lobe subpleural pulmonary nodule (02:33).  The
airways are patent to the subsegmental level.

Limited images of the upper abdomen are unremarkable.

No lytic or blastic osseous lesion suspicious for malignancy is identified.

IMPRESSION:



1. Limited exam.  No evidence of central pulmonary embolism.  Evaluation of
the segmental and subsegmental pulmonary arteries is limited by respiratory
motion.
2. 4 mm left upper lobe pulmonary nodule.
3. Borderline enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes may be reactive.  Correlate
with clinical symptoms.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  Per ___ society guidelines for follow-up of
pulmonary nodules, if no risk factors for malignancy, no followup is
recommended.  If risk factors, recommend followup CT in ___ year.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA chest

INDICATION:  ___ man status post sigmoid colectomy who was reintubated
for respiratory failure and has spiking fevers.  Evaluate for pulmonary
embolus.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of 130 cc of Omnipaque intravenous
contrast.
Reformatted coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal
intensity projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  DLP: 134 2.32 mGy-cm

COMPARISON:  No prior cross-sectional imaging of the chest is available on
PACs at the time of this dictation.  Limited reference is made with the CT
abdomen and pelvis dated ___.

FINDINGS: 

The aorta and its major branch vessels are patent, with no evidence of
stenosis, occlusion, dissection, or aneurysmal formation.  The heart is
enlarged.  Diffuse coronary artery calcifications or moderate on this
nondedicated exam.  Aortic valve calcifications are mild-to-moderate.  Mitral
non was calcifications are mild.  No evidence of a pericardial effusion.  A
right subclavian vein approach PICC tip ends in the distal SVC.

Central filling defects in right upper lobar, segmental, and subsegmental
pulmonary arteries are consistent with acute pulmonary emboli (e.g., series 3,
image 99, 98, 92, 111, 70, 80).  The main pulmonary artery is normal in
caliber.  The right pulmonary artery is top-normal in caliber measuring up to
27 mm.  The left pulmonary artery is normal in caliber.  No evidence of right
heart strain.

Axillary lymph nodes are prominent but not pathologically enlarged.  No
supraclavicular lymphadenopathy.  Multiple mediastinal lymph nodes are
measurable up to 8 mm in short axis in the upper paratracheal station and 11
mm in short axis in the subcarinal station, possibly reactive (series 3, image
57).  No hilar lymphadenopathy.

A trace right pleural effusion is nonhemorrhagic and has markedly decreased in
size since ___.  Associated dependent right lung relaxation atelectasis
is minimal in also markedly improved.  A small left nonhemorrhagic pleural
effusion persists and is minimally changed.  Left lower lobe segmental and
subsegmental bronchi are opacified and obstructed with mucous secretions with
associated heterogeneously enhancing parenchymal opacity that likely reflects
a combination of atelectasis and postobstructive pneumonia given the provided
clinical history.

The patient is intubated with the ETT in standard position.  No evidence of
pneumothorax.

Detailed evaluation of the lung parenchyma for nodules is markedly limited
secondary to respiratory and cardiac motion artifact. A left apex pulmonary
micronodule measures 2 mm (Series 3, image 40; series 601b, image 37).  Linear
perifissural opacity in the right upper lobe is likely atelectasis (series
601B, image 41).

The thyroid gland is normal in size and enhances normally other than a
millimetric a hypodense nodule in the right lobe of doubtful clinical
significance (series 3, image 32).

An enteric tube is incompletely imaged in the esophagus coursing into the
stomach in the left upper quadrant of the abdomen.  This exam is not dedicated
for imaging of the abdomen.  Limited views of the upper abdomen are grossly
unremarkable.

No osseous lesions concerning for malignancy or infection in the chest cage. 
Multi-level degenerative changes of the thoracic spine are moderate with
prominent anterior osteophytes.  No evidence of acute fracture.

IMPRESSION:


1. Acute pulmonary embolus in right upper pulmonary lobar, segmental, and
subsegmental arteries.
2. Mucous secretions obstructing left lower lobe segmental bronchi with
associated atelectasis and post-obstructive pneumonia.
3. Small left and trace right non-hemorrhagic pleural effusions, unchanged on
the left and markedly improved on the right from ___.
4. Interval improvement in right lower lobe atelectasis since ___.
5. At least moderate calcified coronary artery disease on this nondedicated
exam.
6. Cardiomegaly.
7. Aortic valve calcifications.
8. 2-mm left apex pulmonary micronodule.  If the patient is low risk, no
follow-up is needed.  If the patient is high risk, dedicated chest CT in ___
year is recommended.

RECOMMENDATION(S):   If the patient is low risk, no follow-up is needed for a
sub- 4 mm pulmonary micronodule.  If the patient is high risk, dedicated chest
CT in ___ year is recommended.

NOTIFICATION:  The findings were discussed with ___, M.D. by
___, M.D. on the telephone on ___ at 2:15 ___, 1 minutes after
discovery of the findings.  Findings were also discussed with Dr. ___
___ on the telephone on ___ at 218 pm.

A trace right pleural effusion is nonhemorrhagic and has markedly decreased in
size since ___.  Associated dependent right lung relaxation atelectasis
is minimal in also markedly improved.  A small left nonhemorrhagic pleural
effusion persists and is minimally changed.  Left lower lobe segmental and
subsegmental bronchi are opacified and obstructed with mucous secretions with
associated heterogeneously enhancing parenchymal opacity that likely reflects
a combination of atelectasis and postobstructive pneumonia given the provided
clinical history.

The patient is intubated with the ETT in standard position.  No evidence of
pneumothorax.

Detailed evaluation of the lung parenchyma for nodules is markedly limited
secondary to respiratory and cardiac motion artifact. A left apex pulmonary
micronodule measures 2 mm (Series 3, image 40; series 601b, image 37).  Linear
perifissural opacity in the right upper lobe is likely atelectasis (series
601B, image 41).

The thyroid gland is normal in size and enhances normally other than a
millimetric a hypodense nodule in the right lobe of doubtful clinical
significance (series 3, image 32).

An enteric tube is incompletely imaged in the esophagus coursing into the
stomach in the left upper quadrant of the abdomen.  This exam is not dedicated
for imaging of the abdomen.  Limited views of the upper abdomen are grossly
unremarkable.

No osseous lesions concerning for malignancy or infection in the chest cage. 
Multi-level degenerative changes of the thoracic spine are moderate with
prominent anterior osteophytes.  No evidence of acute fracture.
sections.impression:
1. Acute pulmonary embolus in right upper pulmonary lobar, segmental, and
subsegmental arteries.
2. Mucous secretions obstructing left lower lobe segmental bronchi with
associated atelectasis and post-obstructive pneumonia.
3. Small left and trace right non-hemorrhagic pleural effusions, unchanged on
the left and markedly improved on the right from ___.
4. Interval improvement in right lower lobe atelectasis since ___.
5. At least moderate calcified coronary artery disease on this nondedicated
exam.
6. Cardiomegaly.
7. Aortic valve calcifications.
8. 2-mm left apex pulmonary micronodule.  If the patient is low risk, no
follow-up is needed.  If the patient is high risk, dedicated chest CT in ___
year is recommended.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Staging for newly diagnosed right breast cancer with positive
lymph nodes status post bilateral mastectomies with ___ flap.

COMPARISON:  No prior studies available for comparison.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT contrast-enhanced axial images were obtained from the
thoracic inlet to the pelvic outlet.  Coronal and sagittal reformations were
performed.  Additional non-contrast axial images were obtained from the lung
bases to the pelvic brim.

FINDINGS:

CT CHEST:  Post-surgical changes are noted in the bilateral breasts secondary
to bilateral ___ flaps.  A 2.3 x 2.4 cm high-density collection is noted in
the medial aspect of the left breast as well as a second fusiform collection
is identified in the left posterior lateral breast measuring approximately 10
x 1.7 cm, both likely reflecting a postoperative seromas.  Locules of gas and
inflammation are noted throughout the subcutaneous tissues of the abdomen
consistent with surgical history.  The lungs are clear without focal
opacification or suspicious pulmonary nodules.  There is no hilar,
mediastinal, or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy identified.  Multiple
prominent lymph nodes are noted in the bilateral axilla, left greater than
right; however, none meet CT criteria for pathologic enlargement.  Heart size
is normal without pericardial effusion.  The great vessels appear
unremarkable.  The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.  No pleural
effusions are evident.

CT ABDOMEN:  Liver is homogenous in parenchymal attenuation without distinct
masses or lesions.  There is no intrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation.
Gallbladder is unremarkable.  There is no common bile duct dilatation.  The
pancreas and spleen are unremarkable.  The bilateral adrenal glands have
normal limb thickness without convex margin to suggest mass.  The bilateral
kidneys are normal in size.  The stomach, small and large bowel are
unremarkable.  There is no retroperitoneal, mesenteric or portacaval
lymphadenopathy identified.  There is no free fluid within the abdomen.
Minimal atherosclerotic changes identified within the aorta, without
associated aneurysmal change.  The celiac axis and superior mesenteric artery
are patent.  The portal venous system is patent.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  There no suspicious lytic or blastic lesions identified.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Expected post-surgical changes secondary to bilateral mastectomy and ___
flaps including subcutaneous emphysema and two seromas in the left breast.  No
evidence of metastatic disease.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  Trauma torso

INDICATION:  ___ with MVC, polytrauma // trauma

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axial images were acquired through the chest, abdomen and
pelvis following intravenous contrast administration with split bolus
technique.
Oral contrast was not administered.
Coronal and sagittal reformations were performed and reviewed on PACS.

DOSE:  Total DLP (Body) = 1,497 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  None.

FINDINGS: 

CHEST:

HEART AND VASCULATURE: The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without
evidence of acute injury.  The heart, pericardium, and great vessels are
within normal limits. No pericardial effusion is seen.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary, mediastinal, or hilar
lymphadenopathy is present.  No mediastinal mass.

PLEURAL SPACES: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: There is right upper, and right lower lobe atelectasis.  Lungs
are clear without masses or areas of parenchymal opacification.  The airways
are patent to the level of the segmental bronchi bilaterally.

BASE OF NECK: The thyroid is multinodular with a dominant 1.1 cm right thyroid
nodule visualized.

ABDOMEN:

HEPATOBILIARY: The liver demonstrates homogenous attenuation throughout. 
There is no evidence of focal lesion or laceration.  There is no evidence of
intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary dilatation.  The gallbladder is within
normal limits.

PANCREAS: The pancreas has normal attenuation throughout, without evidence of
focal lesions or pancreatic ductal dilatation.  There is no peripancreatic
stranding.

SPLEEN: The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout, without
evidence of focal lesion or laceration.

ADRENALS: The right and left adrenal glands are normal in size and shape.

URINARY: The kidneys are of normal and symmetric size with normal nephrogram. 
Multiple bilateral cortical renal hypodensities are visualized most of which
are too small to characterize though there is a 1.5 cm right upper pole renal
cyst and a 1.3 cm left lower pole renal cysts.  There is no evidence of
hydronephrosis.  There is no perinephric abnormality.

GASTROINTESTINAL: The stomach is unremarkable.  Small bowel loops demonstrate
normal caliber, wall thickness, and enhancement throughout.  The colon and
rectum are within normal limits.  The appendix is normal.  There is no
evidence of mesenteric injury.

There is no free fluid or free air in the abdomen.

PELVIS:

The urinary bladder and distal ureters are unremarkable.  There is no free
fluid in the pelvis.

REPRODUCTIVE ORGANS: The uterus is of normal size and enhancement.  There is a
2.9 x 2.0 cm right adnexal cyst.

LYMPH NODES: There is no retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy.  There
is no pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

VASCULAR: There is no abdominal aortic aneurysm or retroperitoneal hematoma. 
Mild atherosclerotic disease is noted.

BONES/SOFT TISSUE: There is a burst fracture of T4 with anterior displacement
of the fracture fragments and surrounding prevertebral hematoma extending
superiorly to T1 inferiorly to T6.  There is no bony canal narrowing
identified from this injury.  Multiple spinous process fractures are
visualized from the level of T1 through T4.  There is extension of the spinous
process fractures to the bilateral inferior articular facets at T3 and T4. 
There is also small fracture at the base of an osteophyte at the right
superolateral endplate (103:81).  Suspected right first rib fracture
posteriorly (103:71).  No suspicious osseous abnormality.

The abdominal and pelvic wall is within normal limits.

IMPRESSION:


1. Burst fracture of T4 with anterior displacement of the fracture fragments
and surrounding prevertebral hematoma, as described above, with no significant
bony spinal canal narrowing identified.
2. T1 through T4 spinous process fractures with the T3 and T4 fractures
extending to the inferior articular facets bilaterally.  Suspected
nondisplaced right first posterior rib fracture.  Fracture at the base of an
endplate osteophyte at the right superolateral aspect of the T5 vertebral
body.
3. No acute solid organ injury identified within the chest, abdomen, or
pelvis.
4. A 2.9 cm right adnexal cyst can be further assessed by nonurgent pelvic
ultrasound when clinically feasible.

radiology_facts.report_text:
CLINICAL HISTORY:  ___ female with progressive CLL.  Evaluate degree
of adenopathy and splenomegaly.

COMPARISON:  None.

TECHNIQUE:  Non-contrast MDCT acquired axial images of the chest, abdomen, and
pelvis from the thoracic inlet to the pubic symphysis.  Multiplanar
reformatted images were obtained.

CT OF THE CHEST WITHOUT INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:  The lungs demonstrate apical
scarring and bronchiectasis in bilateral upper lobes and lingula. A
peribronchial consolidative opacity is seen in the right middle lobe which may
represent infectious process.  Small 2-mm to 5mm pulmonary nodules are seen
scattered throughout the lungs for which no prior comparison is available.
Coronary artery calcifications are noted.  No pleural or pericardial effusion
is detected. Prominent lymph nodes are seen within the axilla, mediastinum and
right hilum. The largeset lymph node is a conglomerate of soft tissue in the
subcarinal station measuring 31 x 16 mm. Incidental note is made of bilateral
thyroid nodules, one of which in the left lobe shows calcification.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITHOUT INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:  Within the limitations of a
non-contrast exam, liver, adrenal glands and left kidney are within normal
limits. The pancreas is predominantly fatty replaced. The right kidney shows
multiple parapelvic cysts.

The spleen is enlarged and measures 16 cm. Multiple rounded soft tissue
strucutures are seen adjacent to the splenic hilum in the gastrosplenic
ligament which likely represents a conglomerate of enlarged lymph nodes, the
largest measuring approximately 14 mm. Retroperitoneal lymphadenpathy at the
celiac axis is identified as well. No free fluid or free air is identified.
Bowel loops maintain a normal caliber without evidence of obstruction. 

CT OF THE PELVIS WITHOUT INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:  The rectum and bladder are
within normal limits.  The uterus contains a calcified fibroid.  The adnexa
are unremarkable. There is sigmoid diverticulosis without evidence of
diverticulitis. No lymphadenopathy or free fluid is detected.

BONE WINDOWS:  No suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesion is identified.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Peribronquial consolidation within the right middle lobe which likely
represents an infectious process.

2.  Multiple pulmonary nodules scattered throughout the lungs for which no
prior comparison is available. Given biapical scarring an multilobar
bronchiectasis, ___ month follow up is recommended to assess for stability and
evaluate resolution of right middle lobe consolidative process

3.  Splenomegaly and lymphadenopathy, which is in keeping with history of CLL.

4.  Sigmoid diverticulosis without evidence of diverticulitis.

5.  Bilateral thyroid nodules for which ultrasound can be performed for
further evalution.

6.  Right parapelvic renal cysts.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION: Evaluate for cause of diarrhea in a ___ year old male with CLL.

___.

TECHNIQUE: MDCT-acquired images were obtained through the abdomen and pelvis
after the uneventful administration of 130 cc of IV Optiray contrast.  Delayed
images were obtained through the chest. 

CT CHEST WITH CONTRAST:  Apical scarring with associated bronchiectasis in the
upper lobes is unchanged. Multiple 3 to 6 mm pulmonary nodules scattered
throughout both lungs that are unchanged. Bilateral small pleural effusions
are new.  A subcarinal lymph node conglomerate measures 5.1 x 2.7 cm
(previously 3.8 x 2 cm) There are multiple prominent lymph nodes in the
mediastinum the largest of which measures 1.3 cm in the anterior paratracheal
region. There are bilateral thyroid nodules which are grossly stable since
prior exam.

CT ABDOMEN WITH IV CONTRAST:  There is marked splenomegaly measuring up to 22
x 12 cm. The gallbladder is absent but no cholecystectomy clips are visible.
The common bile duct measures up to 1.1 cm with low insertion and dilation of
cystic duct to the papilla. There is equivocal intrahepatic bile duct
dilation. The liver, adrenals, right kidney are grossly unremarkable. The left
kidney is pushed inferiorly by the spleen. Perihapatic and perisplenic edema
is minimal. There is a conglomerate of lymph nodes around the periportal area
extending to the spleen that becomes confluent and extends inferiorly to the
level of the ___, encircling the infgerior and superior mesenteric artery as
well as the celiac trunk proximally without evidence of vascular invasion. At
the level of the ___, this conglomnerate measures 5.7 x 5.4 cm. A 1.2 cm
retrocrural node is noted. The intra- abdominal loops of large and small bowel
demonstrate no abdominal wall thickening, free fluid, pneumatosis or free air.

CT PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST:  The rectum and bladder are unremarkable.  The
uterus contains a calcified fibroid, unchanged.  There is a small amount of
pelvic free fluid noted. Left common iliac nodes are enlarged to 1.1 cm.

Bone windows demonstrate no suspicious lytic or blastic lesions.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Massive splenomegaly and lymphadenopathy have increased, consistent with
worsening CLL.

2.  Stable appearance of pulmonary nodules scattered throughout the lungs with
unchanged biapical scarring and multilobar bronchiectasis.

3. There is low insertion of the cystic duct with dilation of the common bile
duct to 1.1 cm with mild central intrahepatic ductal dilation. There is no
pancreatic duct dilation. Recommend correlation with liver function tests and
if cholestatic picture, MRCP.

5.  New small bilateral pleural effusions.

sections.indication:

sections.technique:

after the uneventful administration of 130 cc of IV Optiray contrast.  Delayed
images were obtained through the chest. 

CT CHEST WITH CONTRAST:  Apical scarring with associated bronchiectasis in the
upper lobes is unchanged. Multiple 3 to 6 mm pulmonary nodules scattered
throughout both lungs that are unchanged. Bilateral small pleural effusions
are new.  A subcarinal lymph node conglomerate measures 5.1 x 2.7 cm
(previously 3.8 x 2 cm) There are multiple prominent lymph nodes in the
mediastinum the largest of which measures 1.3 cm in the anterior paratracheal
region. There are bilateral thyroid nodules which are grossly stable since
prior exam.

CT ABDOMEN WITH IV CONTRAST:  There is marked splenomegaly measuring up to 22
x 12 cm. The gallbladder is absent but no cholecystectomy clips are visible.
The common bile duct measures up to 1.1 cm with low insertion and dilation of
cystic duct to the papilla. There is equivocal intrahepatic bile duct
dilation. The liver, adrenals, right kidney are grossly unremarkable. The left
kidney is pushed inferiorly by the spleen. Perihapatic and perisplenic edema
is minimal. There is a conglomerate of lymph nodes around the periportal area
extending to the spleen that becomes confluent and extends inferiorly to the
level of the ___, encircling the infgerior and superior mesenteric artery as
well as the celiac trunk proximally without evidence of vascular invasion. At
the level of the ___, this conglomnerate measures 5.7 x 5.4 cm. A 1.2 cm
retrocrural node is noted. The intra- abdominal loops of large and small bowel
demonstrate no abdominal wall thickening, free fluid, pneumatosis or free air.

CT PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST:  The rectum and bladder are unremarkable.  The
uterus contains a calcified fibroid, unchanged.  There is a small amount of
pelvic free fluid noted. Left common iliac nodes are enlarged to 1.1 cm.

Bone windows demonstrate no suspicious lytic or blastic lesions.

1.  Massive splenomegaly and lymphadenopathy have increased, consistent with
worsening CLL.

2.  Stable appearance of pulmonary nodules scattered throughout the lungs with
unchanged biapical scarring and multilobar bronchiectasis.

3. There is low insertion of the cystic duct with dilation of the common bile
duct to 1.1 cm with mild central intrahepatic ductal dilation. There is no
pancreatic duct dilation. Recommend correlation with liver function tests and
if cholestatic picture, MRCP.

5.  New small bilateral pleural effusions.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Bronchiectasis and worsening productive cough in a patient with
history of CLL.  Please evaluate for pulmonary pathology as well as reassess
the extent of known lymphadenopathy.

COMPARISON:  ___

TECHNIQUE:  Axial CT images are acquired through the abdomen without
intravenous contrast.  Thereafter, following the intravenous administration of
100 cc of intravenous Optiray contrast, images are reacquired through the
entire torso.  Coronal and sagittal reformatted images were also reviewed.

CT CHEST WITH CONTRAST:  Airways are patent to segmental levels bilaterally,
though note is made of extensive distal small airway plugging with a reference
plugged airway seen in the right lower lobe (3:37).  In addition, there are
numerous areas of ___ type opacity seen bilaterally, though worse in
the lower lobes.  A large amount of apical pleural scarring is redemonstrated
bilaterally, appearing unchanged.  Note is also made of more focal nodular
opacities, such as one in the right lower lobe which measures 5 x 3 mm (3:31),
though appears unchanged since ___.  An adjacent subpleural right lower lobe
nodule is 5 x 3 mm (3:31), again unchanged in total volume from previous
measurements of 4 x 4 mm.

There are small bilateral pleural effusions, and no pericardial effusion.  The
heart is notable for coronary arterial calcifications.  The great vessels are
normal.  Note is made of extensive mediastinal lymphadenopathy, with the
largest being a subcarinal node, which measures approximately 5.4 x 3.3 cm
(3:26), slightly larger since ___ (5.2 x 2.7 cm).

CT ABDOMEN WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST:  The kidneys enhance and excrete
contrast in a symmetric fashion, and the right kidney contains multiple
hypodensities which are too small to characterize, though likely cysts.  The
spleen is markedly enlarged, measuring 16.8 cm in length and contains numerous
focal hypodense lesions with the largest measuring approximately 1.5 x 1.2 cm
(3:50).  The liver is notable for intrahepatic biliary ductal dilation, the
extent of which appears similar to that seen in ___.  Also similar to that
time is extensive soft tissue density nodules which encompass the upper
abdominal aorta as well as the origins of the renal arteries, celiac trunk,
and superior mesenteric artery.  Overall, the extent of this adenopathy
appears similar to that seen previously.  There is no free gas in the upper
abdomen.

CT PELVIS WITH CONTRAST:  The urinary bladder and distal ureter are
unremarkable.  A punctate density near the bladder base (3:107) may represent
a urethral stone.  Note is made of a dual-looped pessary device.  The colon is
notable for diverticulosis.  There is no free gas in the pelvis.  There is no
pelvic sidewall or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

BONY FINDINGS:  There is no suspicious sclerotic or lytic lesion.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Multiple regions of airway plugging and ___ opacities, suggesting
endobronchial mucous plugging with possible endobronchial spread of infection.
2.  Extensive lymphadenopathy with a nodal conglomerate encompassing the
origin of the superior mesenteric artery and celiac trunk as well as a large
subcarinal node.  Those in the abdomen appear similar to that seen in ___ and
that in the subcarinal chain is slightly larger.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  Pleuritic chest pain which radiates to the back, evaluate for the
presence of an aortic dissection or pulmonary embolism.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axial images were acquired through the chest after the
uneventful administration of 100 mL of Omnipaque during the pulmonary arterial
phase of enhancement.  Coronal and sagittal reformations were provided
reviewed.  Maximum intensity projection images were created and reviewed as
well.

DLP: 432.14 mGy/cm 

COMPARISON:  None.

FINDINGS:

Contrast is seen opacifying the segmental and subsegmental arteries of the
pulmonary vascular tree, without filling defects to suggest a pulmonary
embolus.  The aorta is normal in caliber, without evidence of dissection.  The
left common carotid is incidentally noted to originate from the right
brachiocephalic artery.

The thyroid is normal.  There is no supraclavicular, hilar or mediastinal
lymphadenopathy.  The heart is normal in size.  There is no pericardial
effusion.  The trachea is normal in caliber and airways are patent the
subsegmental level.  There is no pleural effusion or pneumothorax.  There is
no focal consolidation to suggest pneumonia.

The study was not tailored to evaluate subdiaphragmatic contents.  A 7 mm
right adrenal nodule is seen on a background of bilateral adrenal nodularity. 
The imaged kidneys, liver and gallbladder are normal.

There are no suspicious osseous lesions.  Bilateral gynecomastia is noted.

IMPRESSION:

1.  No pulmonary embolus.  

2.  7 mm right adrenal nodule on a background of bilateral adrenal nodularity.
This most likely represents an adenoma but is not fully characterized on this
study.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with history of pneumonia treated 1 week ago
who presents with chest pain and elevated d-dimer// r/o PE

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.
Reformatted coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal
intensity projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Total DLP (Body) = 417 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  None

FINDINGS: 

The aorta and its major branch vessels are patent, with no evidence of
stenosis, occlusion, dissection, or aneurysmal formation.  There is no
evidence of penetrating atherosclerotic ulcer or aortic arch atheroma present.

The pulmonary arteries are well opacified to the subsegmental level, with no
evidence of filling defect within the main, right, left, lobar, segmental or
subsegmental pulmonary arteries.  The main and right pulmonary arteries are
normal in caliber, and there is no evidence of right heart strain.

There is a 1 cm left hilar lymph node which is likely reactive.   No
supraclavicular, axillary, or mediastinal lymphadenopathy.

There is no evidence of pericardial effusion.  There is no pleural effusion.

Within the middle lobe and the adjacent right lower lobe is a cluster of solid
nodular opacities and faint ground-glass opacities which likely represent
changes related to pneumonia, given reported history of recent pneumonia
treatment 1 week ago.  The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.

Limited images of the upper abdomen are unremarkable.

Small hemangioma within T9 vertebral body.  No lytic or blastic osseous lesion
suspicious for malignancy is identified.


IMPRESSION:


1. No evidence of pulmonary embolism or aortic abnormality.
2. Cluster of solid nodular opacities within the middle lobe and right lower
lobe likely represent changes related to recent pneumonia.  Please obtain
follow-up chest CT in 3 months to confirm resolution of these parenchymal
changes.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  Follow-up chest CT in 3 months to confirm resolution.

NOTIFICATION:  The findings were discussed with ___, M.D. by ___,
M.D. on the telephone on ___ at 5:00 pm, 1 minutes after discovery of
the findings.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ man status post fall.  Findings concerning for
cerebellar tumor.

COMPARISON:  Chest radiograph from ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector CT scanning of the abdomen and pelvis was performed
after oral and intravenous contrast.  Non-contrast images of the abdomen and
delayed images of the kidneys were also obtained.

CT OF THE CHEST:  An endotracheal tube is seen terminating in the high
trachea.  Within the lung in the right upper lobe, there is a 1.7 x 3.0 cm
mass which extends along the bronchial tree towards the hilum.  Within the
paratracheal region in the AP window, subcarinal region, and in both hila,
there are necrotic-appearing lymph nodes in conglomeration.  In the
paratracheal region they measure up to 14 mm, in the subcarinal region they
measure up to 18 mm.  There are also small prevascular lymph nodes which have
the same appearance.  Elsewhere in the lungs, there are several other
pulmonary nodules, including on images 3:10, 30, 31, 39, 52, and 32. A
nasogastric tube extends into the stomach.  The heart size is not enlarged.
There is a small amount of pericardial fluid. There is extensive aortic valve
calcification as well as coronary artery and mitral annular calcification. The
thoracic aorta is heavily calcified throughout.

There is a right-sided chest tube in place, which terminates in the posterior
region of the hemithorax.  A small hydropneumothorax remains on the right.
Several minimally displaced rib fractures on the right are also noted with
subcutaneous emphysema.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN:  The liver, gallbladder, right adrenal gland, spleen, and
pancreas appear unremarkable.  There is a hypoattenuating 15 mm left adrenal
lesion.

Both kidneys contain cysts.  The one on the left is too small to characterize.
Loops of small and large bowel demonstrate no evidence of obstruction.  There
is a large amount of stool, particularly in the right colon.  There is no
extraluminal air.  There is no ascites.  There is heavy calcification of the
abdominal aorta without aneurysmal dilation.

CT OF THE PELVIS:  There is a Foley catheter within the bladder lumen.  The
rectum appears unremarkable.  There is no free fluid.  There is no
lymphadenopathy.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  There is a compression deformity of the L2 vertebral
body, age indeterminate.  Confluent anterior osteophytes are noted.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Lung mass in the right upper lobe with several other pulmonary nodules
bilaterally as above.  Extensive medial and hilar lymphadenopathy.  Left
adrenal lesion.  All these findings are highly suspicious for metastatic lung
cancer.

2.  Multiple right-sided rib fractures with small right hydropneumothorax.
Chest tube in place.

3.  Compression deformity of the L2 vertebral body.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Evaluation of patient with history of gallbladder adenocarcinoma.

COMPARISON:  CT abdomen and pelvis from outside hospital from ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT-acquired axial images were obtained through the abdomen prior
to administration of IV contrast.  Following the administration of 150 cc of
IV contrast, MDCT-acquired axial images of the abdomen in multiple phases
including arterial, portal venous, and delayed phases as per liver CTA
protocol.  Delayed images were also obtained through the chest and pelvis. 
Multiplanar reformatted images were prepared.

FINDINGS:

CT OF THE CHEST WITH IV CONTRAST:  Post-surgical changes are visualized in the
left axilla and left breast along with a soft tissue nodule in the left
breast, consistent with prior surgery.  Furthermore, post-radiation changes
are visualized in the left upper lobe.  There is a prominent 7-mm precarinal
lymph node (6:19) which does not meet pathologic criteria for enlargement and
there is no pathologic hilar, mediastinal, or axillary lymphadenopathy by CT
size criteria.  Aside from the previously mentioned left upper lobe opacities
suggestive of radiation fibrosis, the remainder of the lungs are clear with no
evidence of nodules or consolidates.  The visualized portions of heart and
great vessels are within normal limits.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITH AND WITHOUT IV CONTRAST:  Post-cholecystectomy changes
are visualized in the gallbladder fossa.  Otherwise, the liver is within
normal limits with no evidence of focal distinct lesions.  The portal vein,
splenic vein, superior mesenteric vein, and hepatic veins are widely patent.

The stomach, visualized loops of small and large bowel, pancreas, bilateral
adrenal glands, and spleen are within normal limits.  Two subcentimeter
hypodense lesions which are too small to characterize are visualized
throughout the interpolar region of the right kidney; otherwise, the kidneys
enhance symmetrically and excrete promptly and are within normal limits.

Atherosclerotic changes are visualized throughout the abdominal aorta, but the
aorta is normal in caliber and contour.  No mesenteric or retroperitoneal
lymphadenopathy.  No free fluid or free air throughout the abdomen.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST:  The visualized portions of the bladder,
rectum, and sigmoid colon are within normal limits.  No pelvic or inguinal
lymphadenopathy by CT size criteria.  No free fluid or free air throughout the
pelvis.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  No suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No evidence of metastatic disease in the chest, abdomen, or pelvis.
2.  No evidence of gross invasion of liver with any focal masses.
3.  Post-surgical changes are visualized at the left breast and axilla with
radiation fibrosis at the left upper lobe.

sections.indication: Evaluation of patient with history of gallbladder adenocarcinoma.
sections.technique:
MDCT-acquired axial images were obtained through the abdomen prior
to administration of IV contrast.  Following the administration of 150 cc of
IV contrast, MDCT-acquired axial images of the abdomen in multiple phases
including arterial, portal venous, and delayed phases as per liver CTA
protocol.  Delayed images were also obtained through the chest and pelvis. 
Multiplanar reformatted images were prepared.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old female with stage IV lung cancer who received prior
palliative chemotherapy.// Evaluate disease burden. Compare to prior studies.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent reconstructed as
contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal,
and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  DLP: Please refer to the separate dose report in the abdomen and pelvis
CT report.

COMPARISON:  ___

FINDINGS: 

Aorta and pulmonary arteries are overall unremarkable.  Left upper lobe
continues to be collapsed due to obstructed left upper lobe bronchus,  series
4, image 124.  Overall the volume of the collapsed lobe is stable since the
most recent prior examination.  Subpleural nodule in the superior segment of
the left lower lobe (03:21) is stable.  Besides left upper lobe bronchus the
rest of the airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally.  New
mild atelectasis and linear opacity in the right lower lobe.  Otherwise no new
pulmonary nodules masses or consolidations demonstrated.

There is no pleural effusion accumulation.  No mediastinal lymph nodes
currently seen with resolution of lymphadenopathy demonstrated on ___.  Heart size is normal.  No pericardial effusion is seen.  The left
thyroid has been surgically removed.

Lytic and sclerotic lesions previously mentioned of the thoracic spine are
overall unchanged.  Multiple compression fractures are stable.  No substantial
retropulsion.  Mid sternal deformity with associated sclerosis is more
apparent since the last examination likely reflecting subacute fracture.

Image portion of the upper abdomen will be reviewed and reported separately.

IMPRESSION: 

Stable appearance of the left upper lobe collapse secondary to obstructed left
upper lobe bronchus.
No new lung nodules, lymphadenopathy or pleural disease in the thorax.
Subacute sternal fracture is more apparent since ___ and new since
___.

radiology_facts.report_text:
COMPUTED TOMOGRAPHY OF THE THORAX

INDICATION:  Status post allogenic bone marrow transplant, evaluation for
infection.

COMPARISON:  CT part of the PET-CT examination from ___.

FINDINGS:  No incidental thyroid findings.  No enlarged supraclavicular,
infraclavicular or axillary lymph nodes.  All visible axillary lymph nodes are
normal in size.  The anterior mediastinum is unremarkable.  In the middle
mediastinum several normal-sized lymph nodes are seen but no lymph nodes are
enlarged.  The ascending aorta is unremarkable, the descending aorta is also
normal.  Normal appearance of the pulmonary artery.  There are moderate
coronary calcifications but no substantial alveolar calcifications. 
Visibility of the intraventricular septum suggests the presence of anemia. 
Normal appearance of the heart.  Normal pericardial fat.  In the upper
abdomen, there is ascites in perihepatic and splenic location.  The left
adrenal shows a large adenoma, unchanged since the previous examination.

There is no evidence of osteolytic lesions.  A solitary lesion of reduced
spongiosa substance, surrounded by sclerotic rim is unchanged since the
previous examination.  No evidence of sternal abnormalities.  No rib lesions.

Mild bilateral pleural effusions, right more than left.  Subsequent areas of
atelectasis at the lung bases, also right more than left.  The opacities
appear atelectatic rather than infectious given their band-like morphology.  

In the right upper lobe, there is an approximately 2 cm big
not-perfectly-rounded solid structure (6, 82) that has direct contact to
multiple airways, a predominantly solid core and a minimal ground-glass halo. 
The lesion also shows areas of decreased surrounding lung attenuation,
potentially reflecting local fibrosis.  Although lesion could represent a
scar, the possibility of a potentially fungal infection must also be
considered.

A second smaller but morphologically similar lesion is located in the middle
lobe (6, 190).  

Irregularities of the airways suggest mild chronic airways disease.  No
evidence of endobronchial lesions.  The airways are patent.  No other
abnormalities in the lung parenchyma.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Right upper lobe and right middle lobe nodule.  The possibility of a
fungal infection should be considered.
2.  Bilateral areas of atelectasis and pleural effusions.
3.  Mild chronic airways disease.  No evidence of lymphadenopathy.

At the time of dictation and observation at 4:58 p.m., the referring
physician, ___, was notified on ___.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ with s/p CVA, p/w acute respiratory distress, dysphagia,
significant upper airiway secretions; intubated for respiratory distress,
hypoxia;  // eval for mechanical obstruction, pna, upper airway inflammation

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.  Reformatted
coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal intensity
projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Total DLP (Body) = 284 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT torso ___

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE: Pulmonary vasculature is well opacified to the
subsegmental level without filling defect to indicate a pulmonary embolus. The
thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without evidence of dissection or
intramural hematoma.  Moderate atherosclerotic calcifications seen throughout
the thoracic aorta.  The heart, pericardium, and great vessels are within
normal limits. No pericardial effusion is seen.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary, mediastinal, or hilar
lymphadenopathy is seen.  No mediastinal mass.  The esophagus is significantly
patulous and filled with solid and liquid material, more solid at the inferior
esophagus just superior to the GE junction.

PLEURAL SPACES:  There are likely trace pleural effusions bilaterally.  No
pneumothorax.


LUNGS/AIRWAYS:  The endotracheal tube tip is approximately 2.7 cm above the
carina.  There are secretions along the superior portion of the ET tube. 
There are diffuse hazy nodular with some ground-glass opacities in the upper
lobes bilaterally.  There are dense consolidative opacities in the lower lobes
bilaterally.  The airways are patent to the level of the segmental bronchi
bilaterally.

BASE OF NECK: Visualized portions of the base of the neck demonstrate
heterogeneous appearance of the thyroid

ABDOMEN:  There is a moderate hiatal hernia.  The remainder of the imaged
upper abdomen is unremarkable besides tiny hypodensity at the upper pole the
right kidney which is too small to characterize but likely a cyst.

BONES: No suspicious osseous abnormality is seen.? There is no acute fracture.
Scoliosis is similar to prior.

IMPRESSION:


1. No evidence of pulmonary embolism or acute aortic abnormality.
2. Significantly patulous esophagus is distended with liquid and solid
material, with more solid components just proximal to the GE junction,
suggesting a component of mechanical and/or functional partial obstruction.
3. Dense consolidations in the lower lobes bilaterally likely represent a
combination of atelectasis, aspiration, and infection.  Diffuse hazy nodular
opacities in the upper lobes bilaterally are likely due to infection and/or
inflammation.
4. There are secretions along the superior portion of the ET tube.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  Worsening respiratory status and increased oxygen requirements.
Evaluate for evolving pulmonary process.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was performed without
intravenous contrast agent reconstructed as contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick
axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  1092.10 mGy.cm

COMPARISON:  CTA chest ___ and chest radiograph ___.


FINDINGS:


There is mild pulmonary edema, improved from ___. This is manifest
as peribronchial cuffing and ground-glass opacities.  Interlobular septal
thickening has improved. A nonhemorrhagic, moderate right pleural effusion is
unchanged from prior while a left pleural effusion has decreased, now trace. A
consolidation at the right lung base with air bronchograms is slightly more
extensive than prior and consistent with compressive atelectasis. The
increasing consolidation obscures the previously mentioned pulmonary nodules.
Mediastinal lymphadenopathy is unchanged and ranges up to 10 mm in the
subcarinal station.

A coarse calcification is seen in the right thyroid lobe. A right PICC
terminates in the low SVC. The heart is mildly enlarged and there is no
pericardial effusion. Hypoattenuation of the blood pool is compatible with
anemia. The main pulmonary artery is minimally dilated, measuring 3.1 cm,
unchanged. The aorta is normal caliber.

The esophagus is patulous but otherwise unremarkable. Calcifications are seen
within a mildly enlarged spleen indicating prior granulomatous disease. There
are no lytic or blastic osseous lesions within the chest.

IMPRESSION: 

1. Improving pulmonary edema, now mild, with an unchanged moderate right and
smaller, now trace, left pleural effusions.  2. Increasing compressive
atelectasis at the right lung base.

anemia. The main pulmonary artery is minimally dilated, measuring 3.1 cm,
unchanged. The aorta is normal caliber.

The esophagus is patulous but otherwise unremarkable. Calcifications are seen
within a mildly enlarged spleen indicating prior granulomatous disease. There
are no lytic or blastic osseous lesions within the chest.

1. Improving pulmonary edema, now mild, with an unchanged moderate right and
smaller, now trace, left pleural effusions.  2. Increasing compressive
atelectasis at the right lung base.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ male with history of MDS, neutropenia, status post
stem cell transplant 6 month earlier.  Now presenting with diarrhea and
hypotension.  Evaluate for intrathoracic infection.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent reconstructed as
contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal,
and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  DLP: 524 mGy cm

COMPARISON:  CT chest dated ___.

FINDINGS: 

The thyroid is normal. Supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal and hilar lymph
nodes are not enlarged. Aorta and pulmonary arteries are normal size. Cardiac
configuration is normal and there is no appreciable coronary calcification.  A
right-sided internal jugular central venous line is noted with the tip
terminating in the right atrium.  No incidental large central pulmonary
embolism is identified.

The airways are clear to the subsegmental levels.  There is no evidence of
consolidation, pleural effusion, or pneumothorax.  A 9 mm calcified granuloma
is noted with the anterior left lower lobe (05:44), unchanged from the prior
examination.  In addition, a 2 mm, perifissural left calcified granuloma
(05:35), and a 2 mm subpleural left lower lobe nodule (05:48) are stable.

No suspicious osseous lesion is detected.

For description of the intra-abdominal findings, please see the separate
report for the CT abdomen and pelvis.

IMPRESSION: 

No evidence of consolidation, pleural effusion, or other intra-thoracic
infection.

and 8 x 8 mm maximum intensity projection axial images.

DOSE:  1113.7 mGy cm (torso)

COMPARISON:  CT of the chest dated ___.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with MDS, immunocompromised with cough and SOB 
// cough and SOB

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was performed without
intravenous contrast agent reconstructed as contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick
axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  DLP: 222

COMPARISON:  ___

FINDINGS: 

MEDIASTINUM: The imaged thyroid is normal.  There is no supraclavicular,
axillary, mediastinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy.  A partially calcified left
hilar lymph node is unchanged.  The aorta and pulmonary arteries are normal in
size.   The heart size is mildly enlarged and there is no pericardial
effusion.

PLEURA:  There is no pneumothorax. Small bilateral nonhemorrhagic pleural
effusions, left greater than right are unchanged.

LUNGS: The airways are patent.  New mass-like opacity in the right lower lobe
with surrounding ground-glass opacity (series 5, image 197).  In review of
prior chest radiographs this mass was not present in ___, favoring
acute infection.  There also new peribronchial ground-glass opacities in the
left lower lobe and right middle lobe (05:120).  Previously seen nodular and
ground-glass opacities most pronounced in the lingula and right lower lobe
have resolved.  Diffuse bronchial wall thickening has progressed.  Multiple
scattered granulomas throughout the lungs.

BONES: There are no destructive focal osseous lesions concerning for
malignancy within the imaged thoracic skeleton.

UPPER ABDOMEN:  This study is not tailored to evaluate the abdomen.  The
esophagus is mildly patulous.  Multiple punctate calcifications in the spleen
are consistent with prior granulomatous infection.  Otherwise the imaged upper
abdominal structures are grossly unremarkable.

IMPRESSION: 

1. New mass-like consolidation with ground-glass halo concerning for
angioinvasive aspergillus infection in this patient with immunosuppression. 
Given the rapid development of this consolidation infection is favored over
malignancy.

2.Previously seen multifocal ground-glass and nodular opacities, most
pronounced in the lingula have resolved.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  History of dyspnea, chest pain.  Please evaluate for PE.

COMPARISONS:  None.

TECHNIQUE:  ___ MDCT images were obtained through the chest after the
administration of IV contrast.  Multiplanar reformatted images in coronal and
sagittal axes were generated and reviewed.

FINDINGS:  The thyroid is normal.  There is no axillary or supraclavicular
lymphadenopathy.  Note, however, is made of enlarged hilar and mediastinal
nodes measuring up to 1.1 cm.  The heart size is normal.  The pericardium is
intact without evidence of an effusion.

CTA:  The aorta is unremarkable without evidence of a dissection.  The main
pulmonary artery is normal in size.  The main, lobar, segmental and
subsegmental pulmonary arteries are well opacified without evidence of filling
defect concerning for a pulmonary embolus.

The airways are patent to the subsegmental levels.  There is a moderate right
and small left pleural effusion.  There is a 4-mm nodule in the right lower
lobe (series 3, image 158).  There is a 0.6 cm x 0.7 cm nodule, pleural-based,
in the right lower lobe (series 3, image 132).  The lungs demonstrate diffuse
septal thickening with peribronchiolar thickening concerning for moderate
pulmonary edema bilaterally.  There is a large left lower lobe granuloma,
which measures approximately 0.8 cm x 0.7 cm (series 3, image 172). 
Additional granulomas are seen throughout the left lung base.  There is a
focal area of consolidation in the anterior segment of the left upper lobe
(series 3, image 93) as well as consolidation in the lingula (series 3, image
125) concerning for pneumonia.  There is no evidence of a pneumothorax.

This study is not tailored for the evaluation of the subdiaphragmatic
structures; however, multiple calcifications within the spleen are likely
secondary to prior granulomatous infection.  Hypodense lesion in segment II of
the liver measures approximately 0.8 cm and is likely secondary to a simple
hepatic cyst.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  No lytic or blastic lesions concerning for malignancy are
identified.

IMPRESSION:

1.  No evidence of a pulmonary embolus.

2.  Consolidations in the left upper lobe, lingula and right middle lobe are
likely secondary to multifocal pneumonia.  Enlarged lymph nodes measuring up
to 1.1 cm in the right hilum and mediastinum are likely reactive.

3.  Moderate pulmonary edema.

4.  Moderate right and small left pleural effusion.

5.  Lung nodules are seen bilaterally measuring up to 0.6 cm.  A six-month
chest CT is recommended for further evaluation to evaluate for stability.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with MDS, pulmonary nocardia tx w/
imipenem/minocycline; eval for interval improvement  // eval interval
pulmonary nocardia

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was performed without
intravenous contrast agent reconstructed as contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick
axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  DLP: 732

COMPARISON:  ___

FINDINGS: 

MEDIASTINUM: The imaged thyroid is normal.  There is no supraclavicular,
axillary, mediastinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy.  A partially calcified left
hilar lymph node is unchanged.  The aorta is mildly ectatic measuring 4 cm and
pulmonary arteries are normal in size.   The heart size is mildly enlarged and
there is no pericardial
effusion.  Left-sided PICC terminates in the low SVC.

PLEURA:  There is no pneumothorax. Small bilateral nonhemorrhagic pleural
effusions, have decreased.

LUNGS: The airways are patent.  Rounded opacity in the right lower lobe
Has slightly decreased in size measuring 24 x 22 mm previously 27 x 29 mm. 
The surrounding ground-glass halo around the opacity has also decreased
locally.  There is new ground-glass and nodular opacity in the posterior
segment of the right lower lobe series 5, image 245.  Peribronchial opacities
in the left lower lobe and right middle lobe have also improved.  Diffuse
bronchial wall thickening has progressed.  Multiple scattered granulomas
throughout the lungs.

BONES: There are no destructive focal osseous lesions concerning for
malignancy within the imaged thoracic skeleton.

UPPER ABDOMEN:  This study is not tailored to evaluate the abdomen.  The
esophagus is mildly patulous.  Multiple punctate calcifications in the spleen
are consistent with prior granulomatous infection.  Otherwise the imaged upper
abdominal structures are grossly unremarkable.

IMPRESSION: 

Slight interval decrease in the right lower lobe nodular opacity,
biopsy-proven Nocardia as well as bilateral small effusions.

New consolidation in the dependent portion of the right lower lobe likely
aspiration given the patulous esophagus, rather than spread of Nocardia.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with MDS ___ allo now with nocardia PNA.  //
Please assess for growth of consolidation.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was performed without
intravenous contrast agent reconstructed as contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick
axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images

DOSE:  DLP: 327 mGy cm

COMPARISON:  CT chest ___

FINDINGS: 

CT CHEST WITHOUT IV CONTRAST: Aside from a rounded focus of calcification in
or adjacent to the right lobe of the thyroid the partially imaged thyroid is
otherwise normal.  Left PICC line terminates in the low SVC. There is no
supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy within the
limitations of a noncontrast enhanced study.  Calcified small lymph nodes in
the left hilum are likely sequela of prior granulomatous infection.  As before
there retained secretions in the patulous esophagus.

Mild cardiomegaly is unchanged with notable enlargement of the left atrium
measures 5.9 cm in AP diameter.  There is no pericardial effusion.  The
thoracic aorta measures 4.4 x 4.2 cm, essentially unchanged allowing for
substantial motion artifact on the prior study.  There is mild dilation of the
right main pulmonary artery measuring 3.1 cm, unchanged.  There is common
origin of the left common carotid and right brachiocephalic arteries.

The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.  There is no pleural
effusion or pneumothorax.  Calcified granuloma in the left lower lobe measures
7 mm.  2 x 1.9 cm right lower lobe soft tissue nodule previously measured 2.5
x 2.3 cm.  Surrounding ground-glass consolidation is improved.  More
inferiorly a previous area of consolidation in the right base is resolved.

There is no evidence of new pulmonary nodule or opacity.  Atelectasis is mild
in the right middle lobe, left lower lobe and lingula.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES: There is no worrisome blastic or lytic lesion.

UPPER ABDOMEN: This study is suboptimal for evaluation of subdiaphragmatic
structures however the following findings are noted.  There is a stone within
the gallbladder which shows no wall thickening or pericholecystic fluid. 
Numerous calcifications in the spleen likely reflects sequela of prior
granulomatous infection.

IMPRESSION:


1. Decreased size of right lower lobe nodule and surrounding ground-glass
opacity compatible with improving Nocardia infection.
2. Right basilar pneumonia resolved.
3. Persistently patulous esophagus with retained fluid places patient at risk
for recurrent aspiration.
4. Unchanged mild cardiomegaly with enlargement of the left atrium.
5. Stable dilation of the ascending aorta to 4.4 x 4.2 cm warrants continued
surveillance.
6. Cholelithiasis

sections.indication:
___ year old man with MDS ___ allo now with nocardia PNA.  //
Please assess for growth of consolidation.
sections.technique:
Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was performed without
intravenous contrast agent reconstructed as contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick
axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images

DOSE:  DLP: 327 mGy cm
sections.findings:
CT CHEST WITHOUT IV CONTRAST: Aside from a rounded focus of calcification in
or adjacent to the right lobe of the thyroid the partially imaged thyroid is
otherwise normal.  Left PICC line terminates in the low SVC. There is no
supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy within the
limitations of a noncontrast enhanced study.  Calcified small lymph nodes in
the left hilum are likely sequela of prior granulomatous infection.  As before
there retained secretions in the patulous esophagus.

Mild cardiomegaly is unchanged with notable enlargement of the left atrium
measures 5.9 cm in AP diameter.  There is no pericardial effusion.  The
thoracic aorta measures 4.4 x 4.2 cm, essentially unchanged allowing for
substantial motion artifact on the prior study.  There is mild dilation of the
right main pulmonary artery measuring 3.1 cm, unchanged.  There is common
origin of the left common carotid and right brachiocephalic arteries.

The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.  There is no pleural
effusion or pneumothorax.  Calcified granuloma in the left lower lobe measures
7 mm.  2 x 1.9 cm right lower lobe soft tissue nodule previously measured 2.5
x 2.3 cm.  Surrounding ground-glass consolidation is improved.  More
inferiorly a previous area of consolidation in the right base is resolved.

There is no evidence of new pulmonary nodule or opacity.  Atelectasis is mild
in the right middle lobe, left lower lobe and lingula.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES: There is no worrisome blastic or lytic lesion.

UPPER ABDOMEN: This study is suboptimal for evaluation of subdiaphragmatic
structures however the following findings are noted.  There is a stone within
the gallbladder which shows no wall thickening or pericholecystic fluid. 
Numerous calcifications in the spleen likely reflects sequela of prior
granulomatous infection.
sections.impression:
1. Decreased size of right lower lobe nodule and surrounding ground-glass
opacity compatible with improving Nocardia infection.
2. Right basilar pneumonia resolved.
3. Persistently patulous esophagus with retained fluid places patient at risk
for recurrent aspiration.
4. Unchanged mild cardiomegaly with enlargement of the left atrium.
5. Stable dilation of the ascending aorta to 4.4 x 4.2 cm warrants continued
surveillance.
6. Cholelithiasis

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  Chest CT

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with MDS now with PNA.  // Please evaluate new
RUL PNA.  Thank you.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT of the chest was obtained from thoracic inlet to upper
abdomen.  Axial images were reviewed in conjunction with coronal and sagittal
reformats

COMPARISON:  ___

FINDINGS: 

New opacity in the right upper right middle and right lower lobe has been
demonstrated with bronchus centric appearance and parenchymal involvement
concerning for extensive infectious process.  The findings have evidence of
air bronchogram as well as ground-glass opacities.  The pre-existing right
lower lobe nodule consistent with known or cardia pneumonia appears to be
unchanged in size.

Patulous esophagus with secretions is consistent potentially with Raf flux in
its unchanged.  Airways are patent.  Minimal amount of left pleural
effusion/pleural thickening is unchanged.

Aorta and pulmonary arteries are unchanged including mild dilatation of both
as well as mild cardiomegaly.

Image portion of the upper abdomen re- demonstrate calcified gallstones and
splenic calcifications.

There are no lytic or sclerotic lesions worrisome for infection or neoplasm.

IMPRESSION: 

Interval development of new multifocal opacities in the right lung as
described.  Differential diagnosis would include bacterial pneumonia,
Pneumocystis carinii pneumonia (less likely) or hemorrhage.  Pulmonary
aspergillosis is another possibility although no specific features consistent
with pulmonary aspergillosis noted.

Correlation with transbronchial biopsy might be considered.

Unchanged appearance of the right lower lobe solid nodule.

Patulous esophagus, potentially representing reflux disease.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with hx of MDS, recurrent pneumonias, admitted
for HCAP improving on imipenem/levofloxacin/posaconazole. BAL culture growing
resistant GNRs.  // Please eval for interval change in multifocal
consolidation

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was performed without
intravenous contrast agent reconstructed as contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick
axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  DLP: 254

COMPARISON:  ___

FINDINGS: 

Significant interval improvement in previously seen multifocal broncho-centric
parenchymal opacities involving the right lung.  Mild residual predominatly
ground glass opacities are seen within the right upper lobe, right middle
lobe, and right lower lobe.  New very mild areas of peribronchial ground-glass
are seen within the left upper and lower lobes. Stable 1.9 x 1.5 cm right
lower lobe pulmonary nodule, biopsy-proven Nocardia.

Multiple calcified pulmonary granulomas, calcified right supraclavicular and
left hilar lymphadenopathy, and multiple splenic calcifications are consistent
with sequela of prior granulomatous infection.

Patulous esophagus retained fluid is consistent  with reflux,
unchanged.  Airways are patent.  Small, nonhemorrhagic, layering, bilateral
effusions, new on the right and slightly increased on the left.  No
mediastinal lymph nodes.  Stable noncalcified hilar lymph nodes.

Aorta and pulmonary arteries are unchanged including mild dilatation of both,
the ascending aorta measuring 4 cm and the pulmonary artery measuring 3.1 cm.
There is mild cardiomegaly.  Left-sided PICC terminates in the low SVC.

Image portion of the upper abdomen redemonstrate calcified gallstones and
splenic calcifications.

There are no lytic or sclerotic lesions worrisome for infection or neoplasm.

IMPRESSION: 

Substantial interval improvement of the multifocal opacities throughout the
right lung with minimal residual ground-glass, can be treated infection.

Stable right lower lobe nodular opacity, biopsy-proven Nocardia.

Ascending thoracic aorta top-normal size, unchanged.  Mild enlargement, main
and right pulmonary arteries, significance indeterminate.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with MDS ___ allo transplant and aspergillous,
nocardia, steno pneumonia.  // Please assess for infection.

TECHNIQUE:   Multi detector helical scanning of the chest was reconstructed as
5 and 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm MIP
axial images.  Contrast agent was not administered. All images were reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 6.4 s, 41.4 cm; CTDIvol = 7.8 mGy (Body) DLP = 319.2
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 319 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Chest CT scanning since ___, most recently ___.

FINDINGS: 

Supraclavicular and axillary lymph nodes are not pathologically enlarged and
there are no soft tissue findings in the chest wall suspicious for malignancy.
This study is not appropriate for subdiaphragmatic diagnosis but shows no
adrenal mass.

There are no thyroid lesions warranting further imaging evaluation. 
Atherosclerotic calcification is mild in head neck vessels and not apparent in
coronary arteries.  Borderline aortic enlargement, 41 mm, is stable since at
least ___.  Mild pulmonary artery enlargement, right 28 mm, also
unchanged.  Evaluation of mild to moderate cardiomegaly should be performed
with dedicated cardiac imaging.  Esophagus is patulous and distended with
fluid throughout the chest to the gastroesophageal junction.  Wall is not
thickened.  .  The volume of fluid retained is greater today than in ___. 
Assessment of function would require a barium swallow.

Tiny left pleural effusion or pleural thickening is stable.  There is no right
pleural abnormality or pericardial effusion.

Central lymph nodes are not pathologically enlarged.  Granulomatous
calcifications are present in the left hilus.

14 x 19 mm right lower lobe nodule, 5:227 was 17 x 19 mm in ___.  There is
no cavitation are extension into the adjacent lung.

Mild bronchial dilatation in the left lower lobe, absent wall thickening,
secretions or peribronchial infiltration is unchanged.  There is no recurrence
of bronchiolitis and no consolidation elsewhere in the lungs.

There are no bone lesions in the chest cage suspicious for malignancy or
infection.




IMPRESSION: 

Fluid retention in the chronically distended esophagus has suggests
dysfunction, best evaluated with a contrast swallow.

Slowly involuting right lower lobe nodule due to Nocardia infection.  No
evidence of new or progressing infection.

Mild left lower lobe bronchial dilatation with no evidence of suppuration.

Borderline aorta and pulmonary artery enlargement, unchanged.  Cardiomegaly
best assessed by dedicated cardiac imaging.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  High risk MDS status post allo ___ year ago with transaminitis and
fever.  Eval for acute process.  Please do with ORAL contrast only.

TECHNIQUE:  Contiguous axial images were obtained through the chest without
intravenous contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformats were obtained.
Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 10.8 s, 69.9 cm; CTDIvol = 7.0 mGy (Body) DLP = 487.1
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 487 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS W/O CONTRAST)

COMPARISON:  CT chest ___

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: 8 mm ovoid calcification posterior
to the right thyroid is similar to prior.  Imaged portion of the base of the
neck is otherwise unremarkable.  No supraclavicular or axillary
lymphadenopathy.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Please refer to concurrent CT abdomen report.

MEDIASTINUM:  No mediastinal mass or lymphadenopathy.  There is oral contrast
and an air-fluid level in the esophagus, findings which are similar to prior.

HILA:  Calcified left hilar lymph nodes appear similar to prior.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: Cardiomegaly is mild to moderate, as on prior.  No
pericardial effusion.

PLEURA: There are small bilateral pleural effusions, slightly greater on the
right than the left.  No pneumothorax.

LUNG:  Evaluation is somewhat limited due to respiratory motion and
inadvertent expiratory phase imaging, which limits detection of small nodules.

1. PARENCHYMA:  1 x 1.4 cm nodule in the right lower lobe (4:1) previously
measured 1.4 x 1.9 cm.  Minimal opacity in the inferior right upper lobe
(4:124) could be localized aspiration or early or resolving infection.  No
focal consolidation.  Calcified granuloma in the left lower lobe (4:187) is
similar to prior.  Other scattered smaller calcified granulomas are also
similar to prior.
2. AIRWAYS:  The airways are patent to subsegmental levels.
3. VESSELS:  The ascending aorta measures up to 4 cm, similar to prior.  The
main pulmonary artery measures up to 3 cm in diameter.  The right pulmonary
artery is mildly enlarged up to 2.7 cm in diameter (4:114), similar to prior. 
There is scattered atherosclerotic calcification in the aorta and imaged
portion of the major neck vessels, as on prior.
CHEST CAGE: No suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesion.  No acute fracture.

IMPRESSION:


1. The previously seen right lower lobe nodule, reportedly due to Nocardia
infection, has further decreased in size.  New small opacity in the inferior
right upper lobe (4:124) could be localized aspiration or early/resolving
infection.
2. Small bilateral pleural effusions.
3. Other findings are similar to prior.
