radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Patient with diabetes and positive blood cultures.  Waxing and
waning chest pain.  Chest x-ray findings concerning for postobstructive
atelectasis.  Please assess.

COMPARISON:  Chest radiograph of the same day.

TECHNIQUE:  Contiguous axial images through the chest were obtained without IV
contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformatted images were generated.

CT OF THE CHEST WITHOUT CONTRAST:  There is a large oval opacity in the
anterior segment of the right upper lobe measuring 5.6 x 2.8 cm in the axial
___ (3:28).  There is opacification/an abrupt cutoff of the medial
branch of the distal anterior segment bronchus of the right upper lobe
(4:104), located immediately proximal to the large oval opacity of the
anterior segment.  The findings are highly suspicious for an endobronchial
lesion or possibly impaction resulting in post- obstructive disease. A mass
comprising the entire opacity would be difficult to exclude in the absence of
IV contrast.

Several additional nodular opacities are seen within the lungs, including a 12
x 8 mm nodule at the right lung apex (4:33), a 7-mm mixed soft tissue/ground
glass in the left upper lobe (4:53), a 4-mm nodule of the posterior right
upper lobe (4:101), a peripheral left upper lobe opacity measuring 6 mm
(4:111), and 3 and 4-mm nodules of the lingula and left lower lobe,
respectively (4:123).  There is patchy dependent atelectasis in both lungs.

No axillary, mediastinal, or hilar lymph nodes meet CT size criteria for
pathologic enlargement.  There is no pleural or pericardial effusion.  There
are coronary artery calcifications involving the LAD and left circumflex.

The exam was not tailored for subdiaphragmatic diagnosis, but the imaged
portion of the liver, spleen, pancreas, and right adrenal gland are
unremarkable.  There is an indeterminate soft tissue nodule that is at the
posterior aspect of the left adrenal gland measuring 1.8 x 1.4 cm, not fully
characterized.  Contrast continues to be excreted from the imaged kidneys
related to CT examination last night.

There are no suspicious osteolytic or sclerotic lesions.

IMPRESSION:

1.  There is strong concern for an endobronchial lesion or impaction of the
medial branch of the distal RUL anterior segment bronchus.  A 5.6 x 2.8- cm
opacity distal to this obstruction may represent post- obstructive
atelectasis, post-obstructive pneumonia, or mass lesion.  These findings would
be better characterized with IV contrast administration. Furthermore,
bronchoscopy may be quite helpful in the evaluation.

2.  There are multiple additional nodular opacities in both lungs.  A sizable
nodule is located at the right lung apex and a mixed ground glass and solid
nodule is in the left upper lobe, and these two lesions specifically are
worrisome for neoplasm.  Consider a PET CT for further assessment.

3.  Coronary artery calcifications.

4.  Indeterminate nodule posterior to the left adrenal, as described on recent
abdominal CT.  Consider a dedicated adrenal CT or MRI for characterization.

Findings and recommendations were discussed with Dr. ___ on the evening of
___.

radiology_facts.report_text:
PROCEDURE:  CT chest with contrast.

REASON FOR EXAM:  Previous right lobectomy for carcinoid.  Assess interval
change.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT chest was performed with IV contrast.  5-mm and 1.25-mm axial
slices were acquired with coronal and sagittal reformats.

COMPARISON:  Previous CT ___ and preoperative CT ___.

FINDINGS:  The patient is status post right upper lobectomy with no evidence
of local distal disease recurrence and unchanged appearance at the right
bronchial stump since the postoperative baseline CT.

A right nonhemorrhagic pleural effusion remains small and slightly less than
on the previous CT.  Linear scarring and atelectasis in the right lower and
right middle lobe are unchanged.

Lung nodules are a sub-2-mm calcified nodule in the right apex (4.52), sub-2-
mm solid nodule in the perifissural region of the left lower lobe (4.143), 4
mm in the left lower lobe (4.153.175) are unchanged since ___.  A 7-mm
nodule in the right lower lobe (4.213) was present in ___ and obscured
by a pleural effusion on the CT on ___.

Small peripheral nodules in the left upper lobe and left lower lobe on the CT
on ___ have resolved and were probably infectious or inflammatory.
No new nodules, mass or consolidation.  The remaining airways are widely
patent to subsegmental level. The aorta, pulmonary artery and heart size is
normal.  No pericardial effusion.  Calcification in the coronary arteries is
mild and unchanged.

Limited views of the upper abdomen are unremarkable except to note nodular
enlargement to the left adrenal gland, unchanged since ___.

No destructive or sclerotic bone lesions.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Status post right upper lobectomy with no evidence of local or distant
disease recurrence.
2.  Stable multiple pulmonary nodules, the largest is in the right lower lobe,
11 mm.
3.  Resolution of multiple peripherally located left upper and left lower lobe
pulmonary nodules were probably inflammatory/infectious.
4.  Stable small right pleural effusion.
5.  Stable enlargement of the left adrenal gland.

radiology_facts.report_text:
COMPUTED TOMOGRAPHY OF THE THORAX:

INDICATION:  History of right upper lobectomy for carcinoid, surveillance
examination.

COMPARISON:  ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Volumetric CT acquisitions over the entire thorax in inspiration,
administration of intravenous contrast material, multiplanar reconstructions.

FINDINGS:  As compared to the previous examination, there is unchanged size
and appearance of the 2 mm calcified lung nodule in the right lung apex (4,
64).

Also unchanged in size and morphology is a 4-mm lung nodule in the subpleural
regions of the left lower lobe (5, 174).  The pre-existing 7-mm right lower
lobe subpleural nodule has almost completely resolved.  At its former
location, a 1-mm subpleural nodule is seen (5, 239).

Several other millimetric and subpleural nodules in both lungs are unchanged
in appearance.  There is no evidence of newly occurred lung nodules.

Status post right upper lobectomy.  Unremarkable postoperative situs. 
Unchanged minimal narrowing of the middle lobe bronchus.  No evidence for
intrabronchial lesions or extra bronchial pathology suggesting recurrence.  No
pathological lymph node enlargement.  Unchanged nonsuspicious borderline lymph
nodes in the axillary regions.

Unchanged right dorsal pleural effusion, no evidence of substantial
pericardial or left pleural effusion.

Unchanged mild-to-moderate coronary calcifications.

No incidental thyroid findings.  No enlarged hilar or mediastinal lymph nodes.
Unchanged subtle enlargement of the left adrenal gland.  Unchanged cyst of the
right kidney.

No evidence of destructive bone lesions.

IMPRESSION:  As compared to ___, there are no relevant changes. 
The pre-existing nodules are unchanged, one nodule in the right lower lobe has
almost completely resolved.  No evidence of recurrence.

Unchanged stable enlargement of the left adrenal gland.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  ___ years old woman with heavy smoking history, recent skin lesions,
?paraneoplastic; low-dose CT for lung CA screening.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was obtained with
low-dose protocol for lung nodules screening during infusion of Omnipaque
non-ionic contrast material agent.  Images were reconstructed as contiguous 5-
and 1.25-mm thick axial helical, 5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 x
8 mm MIPs projections.

COMPARISON:  Exam is compared to chest CT of ___.

FINDINGS:  The multiple bilateral hypodense nodules of the thyroid gland are
unchanged since neck CT of ___, with the largest in the left thyroid
lobe measuring 13 x 18 mm (7:3).  There is no peripheral or central
lymphadenopathy.  Right hilar lymph node is 11 x 18 mm (7:25), was 20 x 18 mm
in ___, AP window lymph node is 13 x 19 mm (9:94) was 10 x 24 mm in
___.  Great vessels are normal.  Heart size is normal without pericardial
effusion.  Aortosclerosis is mild.

There is no pleural effusion.

Even though this exam is not tailored for abdominal imaging, it shows normal
liver, spleen, imaged portion of the kidneys and pancreas.  Stable homogeneous
enlargement of the left adrenal gland, unchanged since ___.

BONES:  There are no bone lesions suspicious for malignancy or infection. 
Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine are mild-to-moderate, with large
anterior osteophytes in the mid thoracic portion (14:33).

LUNGS AND AIRWAYS:  Airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally. 
Upper lobe centrilobular and paraseptal emphysema is stable since ___.  There are no lung nodules suspicious for malignancy.  All the following
nodules are stable since ___ and therefore not concerning for
malignancy (series 9: images 41, 55, 57, 60, 63, 69, 131).  Mild right
paravertebral fibrosis in the basal medial segment of the right lower lobe
(9:248).

IMPRESSION:
1.  There is no sign of intrathoracic malignancy.  Small subpleural punctate
nodules are stable since ___ and not concerning for malignancy.
2.  There is no peripheral lymphadenopathy, central lymphnodes have decreased
since ___
3.  Stability of centrilobular and paraseptal emphysema mainly distributed in
the upper lobes.
4.  Stable appearance of multiple thyroid nodules.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Recent imaging of the neck with enlarged lymph nodes.  Recent
small intracranial hemorrhage, possibly due to underlying mass.  Evaluate for
primary malignancy.

COMPARISONS:  CTA head and neck, ___.

TECHNIQUE:  5 mm axial sections were taken through the chest, abdomen, and
pelvis after the administration of IV and oral contrast.  Sagittal and coronal
reformats were obtained and reviewed.  DLP is 818.90 mGy-cm.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:  There is no visualized supraclavicular or axillary lymphadenopathy. 
Within the mediastinum is a large 40 x 33 mm heterogeneously enhancing lymph
node or conglomerate of lymph nodes (2, 17).  There is a right hilar lymph
node which measures 17 x 15 mm (2, 26).  There are several other smaller
prominent mediastinal lymph nodes that do not meet criteria for pathologic
enlargement.  In the right lower lobe, is a 6 mm round pulmonary nodule
adjacent to the pleural surface.  This is of unclear significance, but could
represent an underlying lung cancer.  There is a benign-appearing densely
calcified 15-mm nodule (2, 39), which is likely secondary to old granulomatous
disease.  There are also calcified hilar lymph nodes on the right, which are
likely from old granulomatous disease.  There are no other nodules seen within
the lungs.  There is no consolidation or pleural effusion.  The heart is
normal in shape and size.  There are sternotomy wires and clips within the
anterior heart.  There is no pericardial effusion.

ABDOMEN:  The liver enhances homogeneously without discrete mass or lesion. 
There is no intra- or extra-hepatic biliary duct dilation.  The portal veins
are patent.  The patient is status post cholecystectomy with clips in the
gallbladder fossa.  There are multiple punctate calcifications within the
spleen, likely from old granulomatous disease.  The pancreas, adrenals, and
kidneys are unremarkable.  The kidneys enhance and excrete contrast
appropriately.  The stomach and small bowel are unremarkable.  There is no
mesenteric or abdominal lymphadenopathy.  The abdominal vasculature is normal
in course and caliber.  There is moderate atherosclerosis.

PELVIS:  The large bowel is unremarkable without mass, wall edema, or
strictures.  The urinary bladder and prostate are unremarkable.  There is no
pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  There are no concerning lytic or sclerotic bone lesions.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Large necrotic mediastinal and hilar lymph nodes.
2.  Solitary non-calcified right lower lobe 6 mm pulmonary nodule.
3.  Calcifications within the spleen, hilum and a pulmonary nodule are
consistent with old granulomatous disease, likely histoplasmosis.
4.  Essentially normal exam of the abdomen and pelvis.

Results were discussed with Dr. ___ telephone at 4:15 p.m. on ___
by Dr. ___.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ male with metastatic melanoma.

COMPARISONS:  PET-CT of ___ and CT torso of ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axial images were obtained from the thoracic inlet to the
pubic symphysis after the uneventful administration of intravenous and oral
contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformations were provided and reviewed.

DLP:  2303.90 mGy-cm.

CHEST:  The thyroid is normal.  There is no axillary lymphadenopathy.  A 3.9 x
2.9 cm right paratracheal node (3:21) is unchanged from prior where it
measured 3.9 x 3.4 cm.  A 1.5 x 0.9 cm right hilar lymph node (3:30) is mostly
unchanged from 1.8 x 1.4 cm.  A 6-mm right middle lobe nodule is unchanged
(3:32).  The 12-cm calcified right lower lobe nodule is also unchanged from
prior (3:45).  There are no new masses or nodules seen.  There is no pleural
effusion or pneumothorax.  The heart size is normal and there is no
pericardial effusion.  Sternotomy wires and postsurgical changes in the
mediastinum are noted.

ABDOMEN:  The liver enhances homogeneously without focal lesions.  The
gallbladder is surgically absent.  Multiple calcified granulomas are again
seen in otherwise normal spleen.  The pancreas and adrenal glands are normal. 
The kidneys enhance symmetrically and excrete contrast without hydronephrosis.
A defect seen in the right lower renal pole likely relates to prior injury
(7:45).  The stomach, large and small bowel are normal.  There is no
retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy.  No free air or free fluid is
appreciated.  A small fat-containing umbilical hernia is noted.

PELVIS:  The bladder, prostate, rectum, and sigmoid are normal.  There is no
free pelvic fluid.  A metallic density in the left groin is again noted.  A
nodule in the left ischioanal fossa (3:120) has increased in size, previously
measuring 7 mm and now measuring 2.4 x 1.9 cm.

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES:  There are no suspicious osseous lesions.  A sclerotic
focus seen in the left acetabulum is likely a bone island.  There are no new
subcutaneous nodules concerning for disease.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Marked increase in size in a left ischioanal nodule, concerning for
malignancy.
2.  Stable mediastinal and hilar lymphadenopathy.
3.  Unchanged 6-mm right middle lobe pulmonary nodule.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAM:  CT torso without contrast.

HISTORY:  ___ man with metastatic melanoma, restaging.

COMPARISON:  CT torso ___, CT torso ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Standard departmental protocol non-contrast CT of the chest,
abdomen, and pelvis was performed.  Patient had no IV access, IV contrast was
not given.  The patient received oral contrast.  Coronal and sagittal
reformats were obtained.  Total exam dose length product ___ mGy-cm.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:  Visualized thyroid unremarkable.  Interval slight increase in size of
right paratracheal soft tissue mass measuring 3.5 x 4.3 cm.  Mild calcific
atherosclerosis of a normal caliber aortic arch and descending aorta.  Mild
coronary atherosclerosis.  Difficult to evaluate hilar lymphadenopathy given
lack of intravenous contrast.  No evidence of significant axillary
lymphadenopathy.  Visualized proximal airway is patent.  Interval development
of multiple new bilateral pulmonary nodules, consistent with progression of
metastatic disease.  The largest nodule is in the posterior right costophrenic
angle measuring 1.6 x 2.0 cm.  Bibasilar subsegmental atelectasis.

ABDOMEN:  Visualized liver, pancreas, bilateral adrenal glands appear
unremarkable.  Mild bilateral perinephric fat stranding, nonspecific. 
Probable simple-appearing cyst measuring 13 mm arising from the anterior
cortex of the interpolar region of the right kidney.  Multiple splenic
calcified granulomas.  Moderate calcific atherosclerosis of a normal caliber
abdominal aorta.  Visualized small bowel unremarkable.  In the right upper
quadrant, are multiple enlarged mesenteric lymph node masses, the largest
measuring 2.7 x 2.0 cm, these all appear new since the prior study, consistent
with interval disease progression.  No evidence of ascites.

PELVIS:  Stool throughout the colon and rectum.  Apparent large soft tissue
mass adherent to the medial wall of the ascending colon, measuring 2.8 x 3.3
cm, this appears new since the prior study.  Normal-appearing appendix. 
Normal-appearing partially full urinary bladder.  Normal-appearing prostate
and seminal vesicles.  Interval increase in size of a left mesocolic soft
tissue mass, now measuring 3.9 x 5.0 cm.  Sclerotic focus in the left ischium
appears unchanged compared to CT torso of ___, most likely represents a
bone island.  Sternal wires appear intact.  Anterior bridging osteophytes of
the thoracic spine.

IMPRESSION:

Interval progression of metastatic disease, including increased size of right
paratracheal soft tissue mass, multiple bilateral new pulmonary metastases,
new right upper quadrant mesenteric nodal metastases, and increased size of
soft tissue mass in the left perirectal mesocolon.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST WITHOUT CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ with  fall from standing, rib pain, headstrike// ? ICH, ? Fx

TECHNIQUE:  Contiguous axial images were obtained through the chest without
intravenous contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformats were obtained.

COMPARISON:  Radiograph from ___

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE:  The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber.  Aortic,
mitral annulus, and coronary calcifications are seen.  The heart, pericardium,
and great vessels are within normal limits based on an unenhanced scan. No
pericardial effusion is seen.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary or mediastinal lymphadenopathy is
present.  No mediastinal mass or hematoma.

PLEURAL SPACES: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: There are multiple bilateral scattered 2-3 mm ground-glass
nodules.  For reference there is a right upper lobe nodule (04:56) and a left
lower lobe nodule (04:59).  There is scattered areas of atelectasis
bilaterally.  The airways are patent to the level of the segmental bronchi
bilaterally.

BASE OF NECK: Visualized portions of the base of the neck show no abnormality.

ABDOMEN: There is a right liver lobe hypodensity, likely compatible with
hepatic cysts versus hamartoma.  Otherwise the included portion of the
unenhanced upper abdomen is unremarkable.

BONES: No acute fracture or suspicious osseous abnormality is seen. 
Degenerative changes are seen in bilateral shoulders.  There are old healed
right rib fractures.  There is a T2 superior endplate compression deformity,
unchanged compared to prior study.  There is a T3 superior endplate
compression deformity not previously imaged though no findings are present to
suggest acuity.

IMPRESSION:


1. No acute fracture or traumatic malalignment.
2. Multiple scattered bilateral 2-3 mm ground-glass pulmonary nodules as
detailed above.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  Based upon ___ ___ society guidelines multiple
pure ground-glass nodules less than 6 mm are usually benign but follow up
should be considered in high risk patients at 2 and ___ years.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  Liver mass on ultrasound.  Staging torso.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
without intravenous contrast.  Reformatted coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial
images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 1.9 s, 30.7 cm; CTDIvol = 4.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 130.4
mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 2.0 s, 26.7 cm; CTDIvol = 23.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 620.5
mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 5.4 s, 71.4 cm; CTDIvol = 21.5 mGy (Body) DLP =
1,531.8 mGy-cm.
   4) Sequenced Acquisition 5.0 s, 20.0 cm; CTDIvol = 46.7 mGy (Head) DLP =
934.2 mGy-cm.
   5) Spiral Acquisition 2.0 s, 26.7 cm; CTDIvol = 23.0 mGy (Body) DLP = 612.5
mGy-cm.
   6) Stationary Acquisition 0.6 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 3.4 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.7
mGy-cm.
   7) Stationary Acquisition 7.2 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 40.2 mGy (Body) DLP =
20.1 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 2,917 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Head) = 934 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS W AND W/O CONTRAST, ADDL SECTIONS)

COMPARISON:  None

FINDINGS: 

FINDINGS:

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: The thyroid is unremarkable.  There
is no supraclavicular or axillary lymphadenopathy.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Please see the report from the concurrent CT scan of the
abdomen and pelvis for abdominopelvic findings.

MEDIASTINUM: The several subcarinal lymph nodes are present, measuring up to
1.1 cm in short axis.  The esophagus demonstrates diffuse esophageal wall
thickening as well as enteric contrast material within it likely reflecting
findings of chronic reflux.

HILA: No hilar lymphadenopathy.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: The heart is not enlarged.  Coronary arterial
calcification are present.  No pericardial effusion.  There is a right
cardiophrenic angle lymph node measuring 7 mm.
PLEURA: There is a small simple density right pleural effusion.  No
pneumothorax.
LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA:  There is right lower and left lower lobe atelectasis. 
Pulmonary nodules are seen within the left lung.  There is a 3 mm nodule in
the left upper lobe (306:48, a 3 mm nodule in the left upper lobe (306:80), as
well as a possible 5 mm nodule in the left lower lobe adjacent to the fissure
(306:117).
2. AIRWAYS:  The airways are patent through the subsegmental level.
3. VESSELS:  There is calcification of the thoracic aorta.  Otherwise no
evidence of aneurysmal dilatation of the thoracic aorta or pulmonary arteries.
CHEST CAGE: There are healing left posterolateral 8 through tenth rib
fractures.  No suspicious osseous lesions are seen within the thoracic
skeleton.

IMPRESSION:


1. 3 left lung pulmonary nodules measure up to 5 mm.  Follow-up as per
clinical protocol is recommended.
2. Diffuse esophageal wall thickening as well as enteric contrast material
within it, likely reflecting chronic reflux.
3. Small right pleural effusion.
4. Healing left posterolateral 8 through tenth rib fractures.

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with metastatic HCC to bone and LN// restaging

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector CT of the chest was performed after the

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:

EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with metastatic HCC to bone and LN// restaging

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was performed with
intravenous contrast agent and reconstructed as contiguous 5-millimeter and
1.25 millimeter thick axial, 2.5 millimeter thick coronal and parasagittal and
8 x 8 millimeter maximum intensity projection axial images.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 4.0 s, 25.9 cm; CTDIvol = 3.4 mGy (Body) DLP = 85.8
mGy-cm.
   2) Sequenced Acquisition 0.5 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 9.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.9
mGy-cm.
   3) Stationary Acquisition 7.9 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 133.9 mGy (Body) DLP =
26.8 mGy-cm.
   4) Spiral Acquisition 3.4 s, 22.3 cm; CTDIvol = 10.1 mGy (Body) DLP = 219.2
mGy-cm.
   5) Spiral Acquisition 10.4 s, 67.6 cm; CTDIvol = 9.5 mGy (Body) DLP = 639.1
mGy-cm.
   6) Spiral Acquisition 3.4 s, 22.3 cm; CTDIvol = 10.1 mGy (Body) DLP = 219.2
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,192 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS W AND W/O CONTRAST, ADDL SECTIONS)

COMPARISON:  CT chest dated ___.

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: Thyroid is unremarkable. No
evidence of pathologically enlarged or growing axillary or supraclavicular
lymph nodes.  A right-sided Port-A-Cath terminates in the proximal right
atrium.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Please refer to same-day CT abdomen and pelvis for detailed
report of subdiaphragmatic findings.

MEDIASTINUM: Grossly unchanged lymph nodes at the subcarinal and
paraesophageal stations, for example the subcarinal lymph node measures up to
11 mm, (series 10, image 46) in does not meet CT criteria for lymphadenopathy.
Scratch

HILA: No pathologically enlarged or growing hilar lymph nodes.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: Cardiac size is normal.  No pericardial effusion. 
Severe calcified atherosclerosis of coronary arteries.  The diameters of the
ascending aorta, aortic arch descending aorta and main pulmonary arteries are
within normal limits.  No evidence of central pulmonary embolism.
PLEURA: No pleural effusion or pleural mass.
LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA:  Interval resolution of previously described multiple larger
pulmonary nodules in the left lower lobe.
A 3 mm pulmonary nodule in the lateral segment of the left lower lung, (series
11, image 237), unchanged when compared to CT chest dated ___.
A 2 mm subpleural solid pulmonary nodule is demonstrated at the apicoposterior
segment of the left upper lung, (series 10, image 19), unchanged when compared
to chest CT dated ___
2. AIRWAYS:  Airways are patent to the subsegmental levels.  No evidence of
bronchial wall thickening.
CHEST CAGE: Multiple osseous metastatic lesion involving the rib and vertebral
bodies, unchanged when compared to most recent CT chest dated ___.

IMPRESSION:


1. Interval resolution of multiple pulmonary nodules in the left lower lobe.
2. Remaining pulmonary nodules in the left lung are unchanged when compared to
CT chest dated ___.
3. Stable multiple osseous metastatic lesions.  However radionuclide bone scan
and FDG PET study is more sensitive in the detection of osseous pathology.
4. Please refer to same-day CT abdomen and pelvis were detailed reported
subdiaphragmatic findings.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  Chest CT.

INDICATION:  ___ with metastatic HCC here w/ confusion.  Evaluate for pleural
effusion.

TECHNIQUE:  Contiguous axial images were obtained through the chest with
intravenous contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformats were obtained.

COMPARISON:  Chest CT ___.  Chest x-ray ___, performed
at an outside facility.  CT abdomen and pelvis ___.

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE:  The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber.  Though not
performed as a dedicated exam, there is no central pulmonary embolism.  Severe
coronary artery calcifications. Moderate to severe atherosclerotic
calcifications of the aortic arch and thoracic aorta.  Otherwise, the heart,
pericardium, and great vessels are within normal limits. No pericardial
effusion is seen.  A right-sided Port-A-Cath tip terminates in the right
atrium.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: Multiple prominent mediastinal nodes, for
example in the subcarinal station (8:139), measure up to 9 mm, grossly stable
from the prior examination.  No axillary lymphadenopathy.  No mediastinal mass
or hematoma.

PLEURAL SPACES: Compared to the prior study, lobulated, right greater than
left pleural effusions are new.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: Calcified granuloma within the left upper lobe (08:48). 
Micronodule within the left upper lobe (8:69).  Mild, dependent, subsegmental
atelectasis. The airways are patent to the level of the segmental bronchi
bilaterally.

BASE OF NECK: A 6 mm left hypodense thyroid nodule appears stable.  Otherwise,
visualized portions of the base of the neck show no abnormality.

ABDOMEN: The liver contour is nodular, compatible with cirrhosis.  The patient
is status post right hepatic lobe resection.  Evaluation of hepatic masses is
not fully assessed on this single-phase study.  Within this limitation,
multiple hepatic masses are again seen, measuring up to 8.1 x 6.0 cm,
compatible with known multifocal hepatocellular carcinoma.  Moderate volume
ascites.  A wedge-shaped hypodensity in the spleen (8:295) could be due to
contrast bolus timing, although a splenic infarct could have a similar
appearance.  Within the left adrenal gland is an approximately 4.3 x 4.0 cm
metastatic lesion, not significantly changed in size from 4.0 x 3.7 cm
previously.  Multiple nodules within the right adrenal gland measure up to 0.8
cm, similar in appearance to the prior study.  A simple appearing cyst in the
right kidney measures 1.7 cm.

BONES: Chronic appearing rib deformity of the right lateral sixth rib. 
Multiple osseous metastatic lesions of the ribs and multilevel vertebra appear
unchanged.  There is no acute fracture.  Oblong soft tissue density anterior
to the T4 vertebral body on the right as likely soft tissue extension from
adjacent osseous metastatic disease.  Soft tissue extension medial to the
right pedicle at this level into the canal is grossly unchanged from prior
exam.

IMPRESSION:


1. New, lobulated, right greater than left, small pleural effusions.
2. No evidence of new or growing pulmonary nodules.
3. Cirrhotic liver, with multiple hepatic masses measuring up to 8.1 cm,
compatible with known multifocal hepatocellular carcinoma, not fully assessed
on this study.
4. New, wedge-shaped hypodensity within the spleen, which could be due to
contrast bolus timing, although a splenic infarct could have a similar
appearance.
5. Stable bilateral adrenal metastases.
6. No significant change in osseous metastatic disease of the ribs and
vertebrae.
7. Other findings, as described above.

radiology_facts.report_text:
PROCEDURE:  CT chest without contrast.

REASON FOR EXAM:  Heavy smoking history, recent history of diagnosis
unresolved with two courses of antibiotics.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT chest performed without IV contrast.  5-mm and 1.25-mm axial
slices were acquired with coronal and sagittal reformats.

COMPARISON:  No previous CT was available for comparison.

FINDINGS:  Dense homogeneous consolidation in the right lower lobe (3.45)
contains multiple air bronchograms. Less extensive right basal consolidation
was present on chest radiographs approximately one year ago. Diffuse nodules
throughout the remaining lungs predominate in the middle lobe, right middle
lobe and right lower lobe with relative sparing of the left lower lobe and
lung apices.  These nodules range in size from 1 to 4 mm.  A right pleural
effusion is minimal, and no pathologically enlargement of mediastinal or
axillary lymph nodes is present.The airways are widely patent to subsegmental
level bilaterally. The aorta, and heart size are normal with no pericardial
effusion. Calcification is severe in the coronary arteries, the pulmonary
artery is normal.

A fatty structure appears adherent to the lateral right atrial wall abutting
an area of atrial calcification could be an interatrial lipoma.
The left lobe of the thyroid is heterogenous and enlarged at 21 x 27 mm.

Limited review of the upper abdomen reveals multiple cystic structures in both
kidneys and high attenuation dependent material in the gallbladder most likely
small stones.

No destructive or sclerotic bone lesions are present.  Degenerative changes
throughout the thoracic spine are moderate.

IMPRESSION:
1)Severe right lower lobe consolidation, progressed or recurrent over a year,
and extensive, new diffuse lung nodules most readily explained by multifocal
bronchioalveolar carcinoma.  Alhernatively, the multiple lung nodules could
also be metastases from an extrathoracic malignancy including thyroid
carcinoma.

2)Diffuse severe coronary artery calcification.

3)Probable interatrial lipoma.

4)Bilateral renal cysts.  Small gallstones.

The case was posted to the critical findings dashboard for direct notification
of the referring physician.

sections.comparison: No previous CT was available for comparison.
sections.findings:
Dense homogeneous consolidation in the right lower lobe (3.45)
contains multiple air bronchograms. Less extensive right basal consolidation
was present on chest radiographs approximately one year ago. Diffuse nodules
throughout the remaining lungs predominate in the middle lobe, right middle
lobe and right lower lobe with relative sparing of the left lower lobe and
lung apices.  These nodules range in size from 1 to 4 mm.  A right pleural
effusion is minimal, and no pathologically enlargement of mediastinal or
axillary lymph nodes is present.The airways are widely patent to subsegmental
level bilaterally. The aorta, and heart size are normal with no pericardial
effusion. Calcification is severe in the coronary arteries, the pulmonary
artery is normal.

A fatty structure appears adherent to the lateral right atrial wall abutting
an area of atrial calcification could be an interatrial lipoma.
The left lobe of the thyroid is heterogenous and enlarged at 21 x 27 mm.

Limited review of the upper abdomen reveals multiple cystic structures in both
kidneys and high attenuation dependent material in the gallbladder most likely
small stones.

No destructive or sclerotic bone lesions are present.  Degenerative changes
throughout the thoracic spine are moderate.
sections.impression:
1)Severe right lower lobe consolidation, progressed or recurrent over a year,
and extensive, new diffuse lung nodules most readily explained by multifocal
bronchioalveolar carcinoma.  Alhernatively, the multiple lung nodules could
also be metastases from an extrathoracic malignancy including thyroid
carcinoma.

2)Diffuse severe coronary artery calcification.

3)Probable interatrial lipoma.

4)Bilateral renal cysts.  Small gallstones.

The case was posted to the critical findings dashboard for direct notification
of the referring physician.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ female, positive sputum cytology and adenocarcinoma
with high O2 requirement.  Evaluate for change following course of antibiotics
for left lower lobe pneumonia.  Please note the patient has a history of right
lower lobe pneumonia.

COMPARISON:  Chest CT of ___ and chest radiograph of ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT of the abdomen was performed without IV contrast. 
Subsequently, 5-mm and 1.25-mm axial slices were acquired through the chest,
abdomen, and pelvis.  Three-minute delayed images were obtained through the
abdomen.  Coronal and sagittal reformats were displayed.

CT CHEST WITH IV CONTRAST:  Again noted is dense homogeneous consolidation in
the right lower lobe containing multiple air bronchograms, which is not
significantly changed from the prior CT.  Also again noted are diffuse nodules
throughout the remaining lungs, predominantly in the right middle, right
lower, and left upper lobe with relative sparing of the apices in left lower
lobe.  There is new ground glass opacity in the left lower lobe medially.
There are several small mediastinal lymph nodes but none meeting CT criteria
for pathologic enlargement.  There is no axillary or hilar lymphadenopathy
meeting CT criteria for pathologic enlargement.  Airways are patent to the
subsegmental level.  

There is a heterogeneous 3.6 x 2.8 x 1.9 cm mass in the left lobe of the
thyroid. The heart is enlarged normal.  Unusal fat density adjacent to the
right atrium and is unchanged. There is no pericardial effusion.  There is
atherosclerotic calcification and soft plaque within the aorta, not
significantly changed from prior.  Calcification of the coronary arteries is
also noted.

CT ABDOMEN WITHOUT AND WITH IV CONTRAST:  The liver, pancreas, spleen, and
adrenal glands are unremarkable.  There is hyperdense material layering
dependently in the gallbladder, likely stones and possibly sludge.  There is
no gallbladder wall thickening or pericholecystic fluid noted.  There is no
intra- or extra-hepatic biliary ductal dilatation.  

The kidneys enhance and excrete contrast symmetrically without evidence of
hydronephrosis or hydroureter.  There are several low-density
well-circumscribed parenchymal and exophytic round lesions within the
bilateral kidneys, some of which are too small to further characterize but
likely represent simple cysts.  The largest cyst is in the interpolar region
of the right kidney measuring 2.8 x 2.9 cm.  Largest cyst on the left measures
2.5 x 2.6 cm. There is a small exophytic 1 cm hyperdense cyst arising from the
upper pole of the left kidney. 

The opacified stomach and intra-abdominal loops of bowel are unremarkable. 
There is no free air or fluid in the abdomen.  No mesenteric or
retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy meeting CT criteria for pathologic enlargement
is noted.

CT PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST:  The urinary bladder, distal ureters, uterus,
adnexa, and rectum are unremarkable.  There is extensive diverticulosis of the
sigmoid colon without evidence of acute diverticulitis.  There is no free
fluid in the pelvis.  No pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy meeting CT
criteria for pathologic enlargement is noted.  There is a surgical clip in the
right groin. Two calcified densities within the left buttock are noted, likely
injection granulomas.

BONE WINDOWS:  No suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesion is identified. 
Degenerative changes of thoracolumbar spine is noted.  There has been prior
right shoulder surgery.

IMPRESSION:

1.  No significant change in severe right lower lobe consolidation and 
extensive diffuse small lung nodules compared to ___, which may represent
bronchoalveolar carcinoma, multifocal infection or metastatic disease with
right lower lobe infection. New ground glass opacity in the left lower lobe.

2.  No significant change in heterogeneous thyroid nodule compared to prior
CT.

3.  Diffuse coronary artery calcification and atherosclerotic plaque within
the aorta.

4.  Cholelithiasis without evidence of acute cholecystitis.

5.  Multiple bilateral renal cysts.

6.  Sigmoid diverticulosis wthout evidence of acute diverticulitis.

radiology_facts.report_text:
PA AND LATERAL CHEST

HISTORY:  Advanced nonsmall cell lung cancer after two cycles of therapy.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of 75 ml Optiray nonionic iodinated contrast agent
reconstructed as contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick axial and 5-mm thick coronal
and paramedian sagittal images compared to CT scanning of the chest, ___ and ___.

FINDINGS:  There has been a generalized improvement in the extent both of
nodulation and, in the right lower lobe, congruent tumor mimicking
consolidation.  See for example, the size of previous confluent opacities in
the posterior basal segment of the right lower lobe, 2:40 today and 2:40 on
___.  The size and numbers of pulmonary nodules have also decreased,
see for example, the largest nodule in the lingula, currently 11 x 11 mm,
2:37, previously 8 x 16 mm, 2:33.

Despite the extensive tumor burden in the lungs, central lymph nodes are not
pathologically enlarged and there is no pleural or pericardial effusion.  A 17
x 26 mm hypoenhancing mass enlarging the left lobe of the thyroid and
displacing the trachea to the right is unchanged.  Mild fusiform enlargement
of the descending thoracic aorta, maximum diameter of 1 mm is unchanged in
size and external contour, but contrast agent reveals very heavy mural
thrombus and/or plaque.  Coronary arteries are heavily calcified and the heart
is moderately enlarged.  This study is not designed for subdiaphragmatic
diagnosis except to note the absence once again of adrenal mass.  Gallbladder
has migrated to a prehepatic location should not be mistaken for pathologic
fluid collection.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Mild-to-moderate improvement over three weeks in the size and number of
pulmonary nodules, still very extensive, and in confluent consolidation of
nodules in the right lower lobe.

2.  Stable left thyroid mass.

3.  Severe coronary and aortic atherosclerotic calcification and plaque.

radiology_facts.report_text:
CHEST CT ON ___

HISTORY:  Non-small cell lung cancer after four cycles of chemotherapy.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent reconstructed as
contiguous 5- and 1-mm thick axial and 5-mm thick coronal and paramedian
sagittal images compared to chest CT scanning since ___, most
recently ___.

FINDINGS:

A 3 cm wide heterogeneous left thyroid mass, displacing the trachea to the
right and narrowing it minimally, is unchanged since at least ___.  There is no pathologic enlargement of central lymph nodes, ranging in
size up to 8 mm in the right lower paratracheal station, unchanged since
___.  Regression of widespread bronchioloalveolar cell carcinoma
continues, evident in the previous global consolidation of the right lower
lobe on ___, that gave way to minimal aeration on ___, now
still grossly abnormal but showing more aeration.  Similarly widespread
bronchogenic dissemination producing bronchioliar and acinar size nodules in
all of the other segments of the lung below the level of the aortic arch is
less pronounced.  There is no pleural or pericardial effusion.  Severe
atherosclerotic calcification and mural thrombus in the mildly dilated
descending thoracic aorta is unchanged.  Also unchanged is the mildly dilated
and dissected upper abdominal aorta, maximum diameter 28 mm today, 27 mm on
___ at the level of the pancreatic tip.  There is no evidence of
pneumonia or pulmonary edema.  The heart remains severely enlarged. 
Atherosclerotic calcification is also severe in the left anterior descending
and right coronary arteries.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Continued interval improvement in still severe widespread bronchogenic
dissemination of bronchoalveolar cell carcinoma.
2.  Severe stable atherosclerotic and mural thrombus involvement of the
thoracic aorta and stable abdominal aortic dissection.
3.  Stable severe cardiomegaly.
4.  Stable large left thyroid nodule.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ woman with bronchoalveolar cell carcinoma and recent
chemotherapy, please evaluate interval change.

Comparison is made to the prior study of ___ and ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial MDCT images of the chest were obtained with no contrast
administration.  Sagittal and coronal reformatted images were then acquired.

FINDINGS:  3-cm heterogeneous left thyroid mass, displacing the trachea to the
right and narrowing it minimally, is unchanged since ___.  No
pathologically enlarged node is noted, the largest node in the anterior right
paratracheal station measures 8 mm.  There has been some improvement in
consolidation of the superior segment of right lower lobe and centrilobular
nodules of the left lower lobe, suggesting interval improvement of either
bronchoalveolar carcinoma or infection. The remainder of the consolidative
changes affecting  affecting the right lower lobe and centrilobular nodules in
the right middle lobe and left lower lobe and the left upper lobe are
unchanged. No pleural or pericardial effusion is noted.  

Severe atherosclerotic calcification and mural thrombus in mildly dilated
descending thoracic aorta is unchanged.  Moderately enlarged heart is
unchanged.  Stable right atrial lipoma measuring 10 x 16 mm is unchanged. 
Severe atherosclerotic calcification of the left anterior descending coronary
artery is unchanged.  Extensive infradiaphragmatic aortic calcification with
displacement of the intima is unchanged (series 2, image 61).  The 24-mm right
renal cyst and the 19-mm left internal cysts are unchanged.  The gallbladder,
liver, spleen, and adrenal gland appear unremarkable.

BONE WINDOWS:  No concerning lytic or sclerotic lesion is noted.  Moderate
spondylosis of the spine is noted.  Median sternotomy wires are noted.

IMPRESSION:

1.  Interval improvement in the consolidation of the superior segment of the
right lower lobe and nodular densitiesof the left lower lobe could be
improvement in otherwise, still widely disseminated BAC or in pneumonia.

2.  Unchanged large left thyroid mass.

3.  Stable moderate emphysema.

4.  Stable renal cysts.

5.  Chronic mural thrombus or plaque abdominal aorta.

6.  Longstanding right atrial lipoma.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ woman with known stage IV lung cancer presents with
fever and cough.  Study to evaluate for right lower lobe infiltrate.

COMPARISON:  CT chest from ___, and ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT of the chest was performed without contrast administration. 
Axial images were displayed at 5-mm and 1-mm collimation.  Multiplanar
reformations were generated.

CT CHEST WITHOUT CONTRAST:  A 2.4 x 1.8 cm hypoattenuating left lobe thyroid
mass (2, 2) appears slightly different in configuration but stable in size as
compared to ___.  No new thyroid mass is seen within the
visualized portions of the gland.  

The heart is enlarged.  Multivessel coronary calcifications are noted.  The
previously described interatrial lipoma is unchanged.  The pulmonary artery is
top normal in size.  There is no pericardial effusion.   Small scattered
mediastinal nodes in the AP window and paratracheal stations are not
pathologically enlarged by CT size criteria.  There is no significant hilar,
or axillary lymphadenopathy.  A prominent thoracic aorta demonstrates moderate
calcifications involving the ascending aorta, the arch, arch, as well as the
descending aorta without aneurysmal dilatation.  The thoracic aortic
intramural thrombus as identified on ___, contrast-enhanced study
cannot be assessed on current noncontrast study.  Punctate calcifications are
noted in the arch vessel origins.  

There is subtle and diffuse increase in parenchymal attenuation in the lungs. 
Moderate centrilobular emphysema is present.  There are bronchial and
peribronchial nodules with ground-glass halos, predominating upper and middle
lobes, more severe as compared to ___.  These changes also involve
the right lower lobe, consistent with an infectious process.  In addition, the
dorsal right lower lobe reticular opacities interspersed with areas of
consolidation appear similar to ___, favoring a chronic process.  There
is no pleural effusion.  Central airways are patent.

Limited subdiaphragmatic evaluation reveals stable multiple bilateral renal
cysts.  A sub-2-mm nonobstructive right renal stone is unchanged.  Symmetric
lateral conal fascial thickening is not changed since prior exams.  Extensive
infradiaphragmatic aortic calcifications are again noted.  Displacement of
intimal calcification suggestive of chronic abdominal aortic dissection (2,
54) is unchanged.  

Moderate spondylosis, disc space narrowing and vacuum phenomenon are noted in
the lower thoracic and upper lumbar spine.  Median sternotomy wires are noted.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Increased bronchial and peribronchial nodules with ground-glass halos
since ___, predominating upper and middle lobes, most consistent
with infection.
2.  Stable right lower lobe reticular opacities with areas of consolidation
and widespread nodules predominantly involving the right upper and middle
lobes, consistent with known bronchioalveolar cell carcinoma.
3.  Stable large left thyroid mass.
4.  Stable abdominal aortic intimal calcification displacement.
5.  Stable renal cysts.  Miniscule nonobstructive right renal calculus.
6.  Moderate emphysema.

5.  Stable renal cysts.  Miniscule nonobstructive right renal calculus.
6.  Moderate emphysema.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  ___ female with stage IV non-small cell lung cancer, with
initial sputum cytology showing adenocarcinoma, status post chemotherapy
complicated by renal failure.  Here to reassess disease.

COMPARISON:  CT chest most recently ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axial imaging was performed through the chest without
administration of IV contrast.  Axial images were displayed using 5- and
1.25-mm collimation.  Multiplanar reformatted images were also obtained.

CT CHEST WITHOUT IV CONTRAST:  The central airways remain patent.  Moderate
centrilobular emphysema with upper lobe predominance, as well as minimal
biapical scarring, are unchanged.  Consolidation involving the medial, lateral
and posterior basal segments of the right lower lobe has increased in density
since ___.  Additionally, there is worsening of peribronchiolar
ground-glass opacities in the lingula.  Peribronchiolar ground-glass opacities
in the right middle lobe and right upper lobe are similar to before.  A few
new small foci of peribronchiolar ground-glass opacities in the left upper
lobe (3:20) and left lower lobe (3:35, 32).  

Again note is made of large left thyroid nodule measuring approximately 2.6 x
3.8 x 2.3 cm, with punctate calcification (2:7).  No mediastinal, hilar or
axillary adenopathy is noted by size criteria.  Extensive coronary artery
calcifications are noted in addition to graft or stent in the region of the
left anterior descending artery.  The patient has had prior median sternotomy.
There is no pericardial or pleural effusion.  Mural calcifications are
redemonstrated along the thoracic aorta; however extent of previously seen
non-calcified mural thrombus or plaque cannot be assessed on this non-contrast
study (see CT torso of ___.

While the current study is not tailored for subdiaphragmatic diagnosis, note
is made of unchanged coarse calcification along/within the left adrenal gland.
Bilateral upper pole renal cysts are incompletely visualized.  Also mural
thrombus or plaque within the abdominal aorta is not assessed without IV
contrast.

Degenerative changes are noted in the visualized thoracolumbar spine.  No
region of bony destruction is seen concerning for malignancy.

IMPRESSIONS:

1.  Increased density of right lower lobe consolidation and worsened
peribronchiolar ground-glass opacities in the lingula are most consistent with
worsening of widely disseminated non-small cell lung cancer, much less likely
due to infection.

2.  Large left thyroid mass with punctate calcification redemonstrated.

3.  Moderate emphysema.

4.  Mural thrombus or plaque previously demonstrated in descending thoracic
and abdominal aorta currently not assessed without IV contrast.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Non-small cell lung carcinoma, adenocarcinoma cytology.  Stage IV
due to multiple intrapulmonary lesions.  Initially treated with chemotherapy,
progression on treatment.  Chemotherapy concluded ___.  Ex-smoker. 
Lower back pain and lower extremity fatigue.

ACQUISITION:  Volumetric multidetector CT acquisition of the chest was
performed without intravenous or oral contrast.  Images are presented for
display in the axial plane as 1.25-mm and 5-mm collimation.  A series of
multiplanar reformation images were also submitted for review.

COMPARISON: CT thoraces dating back to ___ and most recently ___.

FINDINGS: The 28 x 33 mm enlarged left lobe of thyroid is stable since
___.  Associated stable right tracheal deviation is mild.  There is no
pathologic enlargement of the mediastinal, axillary, or supraclavicular lymph
nodes.  

Atherosclerotic calcification of the aortic arch, coronary arteries,
descending thoracic aorta is moderately severe.  Mitral annulus and focal
right atrial mural calcifications are moderately severe.  There is no
pericardial effusion.  Mild cardiac enlargement is stable since ___.  The
interatrial lipoma is unchanged since ___.  Median sternotomy wires are
satisfactory in appearance and unchanged since ___.  

The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.  Bilateral apical pleural
thickening is mild stable since ___.  Centrilobular emphysema is
moderately extensive.  There is no pleural effusion.  Subpleural triangular
opacities in the anterior segment of the right upper lobe are stable since
___ but new since ___.  Ground-glass opacities and multifocal
consolidation throughout both lungs have slightly progressed since ___,
for example, cluster of mixed solid and ground-glass opacity in the anterior
segment of the left upper lobe (4A:101) is larger measuring 11 x 11 mm in
aggregate diameter, compared to 10 x 11 mm with overall increased density. 
Consolidation in the lingula, right middle and right lower lobes has also
progressed (4A:142), compared to (4:140) on prior study.  

The study is not tailored for evaluation of the upper abdomen, only to confirm
bilateral renal cysts, stable since ___, atherosclerotic calcification of
the splenic artery and abdominal aorta.  Generalized osteopenia and anterior
osteophytosis of the mid-to-lower thoracic spine are moderately severe.  There
is no CT evidence of malignant involvement of the visualized thoracic spine.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Moderate progression over 7 weeks of severe bronchoalveolar carcinoma
relative to the more pronounced progression between ___ and ___,
specifically an increase in right middle, right lower, and lingular
consolidation and multifocal ground-glass and solid nodules.  
2.  No CT evidence of thoracic vertebral involvement.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST WANDW/O CANDRECONS, NON-CORONARY

INDICATION:  History: ___ with resp failure, unresponsive.  recent cr 0.53  //
PE?

TECHNIQUE:  Contiguous axial multidetector CT images through the chest during
rapid administration of intravenous contrast as per ___ PE protocol.
Multiplanar reformations.

Total DLP: 654 mGy-cm

COMPARISON:  None

FINDINGS: 

CTA Chest: The pulmonary arteries are well opacified to the subsegmental level
and there is no filling defect to suggest pulmonary embolism. The aortic arch
and LAD predominant coronary artery calcifications are noted. There is no
evidence of aortic dissection.  The ascending aorta is mildly dilated to 4 cm.

CT Chest: Thyroid enhances homogeneously. Axillary, mediastinal, and hilar
lymph nodes do not meet CT size criteria for lymphadenopathy. Heart is normal
size. There is no pericardial effusion.

Endotracheal tube terminates approximately 7 cm above subcarinal node should
be advanced by 2 cm for more optimal placement.  The airways are patent to
subsegmental level and notable for moderate bronchiectasis with lower lobe
predominance. There is also moderate centrilobular emphysema. A cluster of
multiple solid pulmonary nodule in an area of parenchymal distortion is
present in the right upper lobe, the largest measuring 11 x 6 mm (3:54). Two
left upper lobe pulmonary nodules measure up to 8 x 6 mm (3:81, 03:26).
Bibasilar opacities likely reflect atelectasis.  There is bilateral lower lobe
bronchiectasis.  1There is no pleural effusion. Pleural calcifications are
present along the posterior lower lobes.

Limited view of the upper abdomen is notable for a stone filled gallbladder.
NG tube is within the stomach.

No concerning lytic or sclerotic osseous lesion is present.  Age indeterminate
compression deformities are present in T3, T6, T7, T9, and T12 without
evidence of retropulsion.

IMPRESSION: 

1. Endotracheal tube terminates approximately 7 cm above subcarinal node
should be advanced by 2 cm for more optimal placement.

2.  No evidence of pulmonary embolism.

3.  Mildly dilated ascending aorta to 4 cm.

4.  Multiple solid pulmonary nodules warrant followup in 3 months to assess
for stability or resolution.

5.  Age-indeterminate compression deformities in T3, T6, T7, T9, and T12
without evidence of retropulsion.

6.  Cholelithiasis, gallbladder full of stones.
